summary: The Electron shell is the most mature, production-minded part of the codebase reviewed: it spawns a two-runtime sidecar (uvicorn backend on :8000 + bundled portable Node serving Next standalone on :3947), polls both health endpoints, auto-restarts each service with exponential backoff and a stable-uptime budget, holds a single-instance lock, runs a psutil-based orphan cleanup on launch and shutdown, ships a system tray with close-to-tray, wires a native multi-select video picker over a contextIsolated bridge, seeds Supabase credentials into %APPDATA% on first run, rotates logs, and has a working electron-updater flow. The big gaps are desktop-feel polish (no splash/progress window during a 30-60s cold start, no code signing so users hit SmartScreen), a UI-only auth gate (1234/1234) while a real LicenseService exists but is never enforced at startup, and a very heavy footprint (full venv + portable Node + ffmpeg shipped as extraResources). For a one-time-purchase consumer desktop app aimed at non-technical sellers, the engineering is solid but the first-run/install/update experience is not yet at the level of a polished native app.
user_flow: User downloads editfactory-setup-x.x.x.exe (or portable EXE) from a GitHub draft release. On launch Electron acquires the single-instance lock, runs cleanupOrphans() (kills anything on 8000/3947), creates a hidden 1400x900 window plus tray icon, seeds %APPDATA%/EditFactory/.env from bundled credentials.env, then spawns uvicorn and the Node frontend. For up to 60s the window stays hidden while the launcher polls /api/v1/health and the frontend root every 500ms; the only progress signal is the tray tooltip ("API starting... / UI ready"). Once both are up, checkStartupState() calls /desktop/auth/status and routes to /login (test gate 1234/1234) or the app. Closing the window hides to tray rather than quitting; Quit from the tray triggers graceful cleanup (kill children + psutil sweep + 500ms settle). If the backend dies it auto-restarts up to 3x; after that a dialog offers Restart/Quit. Auto-update downloads in the background and prompts Restart Now/Later when ready.

features_present.name: Two-runtime sidecar spawn with health-gated window
features_present.description: main.js:184-328,390-426 spawns uvicorn + bundled portable Node, polls /api/v1/health and frontend root every 500ms up to 60s, shows the window only when both are ready. Clean separation of dev (system node/venv) vs packaged (resourcesPath) paths.

features_present.name: Service auto-restart with exponential backoff
features_present.description: main.js:224-271,309-327 restarts backend/frontend up to 3x (1s/2s/4s), resets the counter after 60s stable uptime, and after exhaustion shows a Restart/Quit dialog for the backend. Genuinely good crash resilience.

features_present.name: Single-instance lock + second-instance focus
features_present.description: main.js:114-132 bails before cleanupOrphans on a second launch (preventing it from killing the first instance's backend) and surfaces the existing window on relaunch.

features_present.name: Orphan/port cleanup via psutil
features_present.description: main.js:135-148 + service.py:16-57 kill whatever holds 8000/3947 (incl. child trees) on launch and shutdown. Pragmatic recovery from hard crashes.

features_present.name: System tray with close-to-tray lifecycle
features_present.description: main.js:429-473,541-646 keeps the app alive in tray on window close, tray tooltip reflects startup progress, double-click/menu reopen. Tray image loaded defensively to avoid the Electron 34 throw-on-missing-icon.

features_present.name: Native file-picker bridge
features_present.description: preload.js exposes window.editFactory.selectVideoFiles over contextIsolation:true; main.js:499-520 validates sender origin (localhost only) and returns absolute paths. Correct security posture.

features_present.name: Auto-update (electron-updater)
features_present.description: main.js:587-639 background-downloads from GitHub releases and prompts Restart Now/Later; autoInstallOnAppQuit=false so install timing is user-controlled. Functional but no in-app progress UI and ships unsigned.

features_present.name: First-run credential seeding
features_present.description: main.js:156-181 + before-pack.js copy anon Supabase creds into %APPDATA%/.env once, idempotently, so the packaged app reaches the same cloud as web without a committed .env.

features_present.name: Crash diagnostics logging
features_present.description: main.js:64-93 tees backend/frontend stdout+stderr to userData/logs/editfactory.log with 5MB rotation (one generation). Essential field debugging; dialogs cite the log path.

features_present.name: Backend startup recovery + cleanup
features_present.description: main.py:251-335 lifespan recovers stuck projects/clips/jobs, marks stale jobs failed, purges old jobs, cleans temp/output files, enlarges the threadpool, and starts periodic trash cleanup. Strong server-side resilience that complements the shell.

features_present.name: Desktop auth gate
features_present.description: routes.py:202-227 username/password gate (default 1234/1234) persisted to config.json; checkStartupState() routes to /login. Explicitly a temporary UI gate, not security — backend API auth is fully bypassed in desktop_mode.

features_present.name: License service
features_present.description: routes.py:45-83 expose activate/status/validate backed by a LicenseService writing license.json. Endpoints exist but are NOT enforced at startup (checkStartupState only checks the 1234 login), so the one-time-purchase business model has no actual gate yet.

strengths.point: Crash resilience is genuinely well thought out: exponential-backoff restarts with a stable-uptime budget, orphan cleanup on both launch and shutdown, single-instance lock that avoids self-fratricide, and a backend lifespan that recovers stuck jobs/projects on every boot.
strengths.why_it_matters: A two-process sidecar app is the hardest desktop pattern to keep alive; this handles the common failure modes (hard crash leaving orphan ports, backend death mid-render) better than most indie Electron apps and avoids the classic 'opens but no window' zombie.

strengths.point: Security posture of the native bridge is correct: contextIsolation:true, no nodeIntegration, a minimal preload surface, and sender-origin validation on the IPC handler.
strengths.why_it_matters: Many Electron apps leak full Node into the renderer; this one is locked down, which matters because the app handles a user's local filesystem and API keys.

strengths.point: Field observability: every backend/frontend line is teed to a rotating log in userData, and every error dialog cites the log path.
strengths.why_it_matters: For a BYOAK product sold to non-technical users with no telemetry backend, a local log file is the only realistic support channel.

strengths.point: Clean dev/packaged duality across every path (python/uvicorn/node/icon/credentials), with the frontend on an uncommon port (3947) specifically to avoid colliding with other local dev servers.
strengths.why_it_matters: Reduces the most common 'works on my machine, breaks when packaged' class of bugs and avoids killing an unrelated dev server on 3000.

ux_friction.issue: No splash/progress window during the 30-60s cold start — the window is hidden and the ONLY feedback is a tray tooltip the user is unlikely to be hovering over.
ux_friction.evidence: main.js:527 show:false; window only shown after waitForServices() (main.js:687-696); progress lives in tray.setToolTip (main.js:404-411).
ux_friction.fix_idea: Show a lightweight branded splash BrowserWindow immediately on whenReady with a determinate-ish progress bar ('Starting engine... / Starting UI...') driven by the same poll, then swap to the main window. This single change is the biggest perceived-quality win.

ux_friction.issue: App ships unsigned (NSIS + portable, no win.certificateFile / signing config), so every user hits a Windows SmartScreen 'unknown publisher' wall on install and on each auto-update.
ux_friction.evidence: package.json:21-63 has no codeSigning/certificate fields; requestExecutionLevel user portable; provider github releaseType draft.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add an EV/OV code-signing cert (or at minimum Azure Trusted Signing) to electron-builder. For a $79-149 paid product, an 'unknown publisher' warning kills install-conversion and erodes trust.

ux_friction.issue: Auto-update has no visible UI during checking/downloading — all events only console.log, so a multi-hundred-MB update downloads silently with no progress and no way to trigger a manual check.
ux_friction.evidence: main.js:594-609 download-progress/checking/available only console.log; only update-downloaded shows a dialog. No 'Check for updates' menu item.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Surface download progress (tray tooltip or a small toast), add a tray/menu 'Check for updates...' item, and show 'You are up to date' feedback so users trust the updater.

ux_friction.issue: Desktop login is a hardcoded 1234/1234 gate and the real LicenseService is never enforced at startup, so there is currently no functioning purchase/license gate for the one-time-purchase model.
ux_friction.evidence: routes.py:178-210 test gate; checkStartupState (main.js:370-388) only checks /desktop/auth/status, never /license/validate.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Wire checkStartupState (or a frontend guard) to call /license/validate and route to an activation screen when unlicensed; keep the 1234 gate behind a dev flag only.

ux_friction.issue: Backend port is hardcoded to 8000 and cleanupOrphans kills whatever is listening there — if a user runs another tool on 8000, EF silently kills it (and vice-versa, EF can't start if 8000 is held by a process it can't kill).
ux_friction.evidence: main.js:54 BACKEND_PORT=8000; cleanupOrphans (main.js:135-148) kills by port unconditionally; frontend already moved off 3000 for exactly this reason (comment main.js:55-58).
ux_friction.fix_idea: Dynamically pick a free backend port (probe, then pass to uvicorn and the frontend's API base URL) instead of a fixed 8000, or at least verify the process on 8000 is actually an EF process before killing it.

ux_friction.issue: 60s startup timeout produces a raw error string with no remediation path; on a slow first run (cold venv import, AV scanning) a legitimate launch can hit it.
ux_friction.evidence: main.js:417-422 rejects with a bare message; whenReady catch shows showErrorBox with err.stack (main.js:701-707).
ux_friction.fix_idea: Raise/parametrize the timeout for first run, and make the failure dialog offer 'View Log / Retry' instead of dumping a stack trace at a non-technical user.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Installer/footprint is very large: extraResources ships the entire venv plus a portable Node runtime plus ffmpeg/ffprobe.
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: package.json:85-114 copies ../venv (with many heavy excludes like torch/whisper/TTS still implying a big base), ../frontend/.next/standalone, ../app, and resources/node.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: Download/install size likely hundreds of MB to >1GB, slowing first-time conversion, making every auto-update a large re-download (no delta if unsigned/blockmap issues), and increasing AV-scan time on launch which compounds the startup-timeout risk.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: In-memory pipeline state is lost on backend restart, and the shell's own auto-restart will silently wipe Step 2-3 work without warning the user.
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: Backend restart loop (main.js:224-271) restarts uvicorn on crash; pipeline working state is in-memory per CLAUDE.md/known pain points; lifespan recovers projects/clips/jobs but not live pipeline previews.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: At higher usage (longer sessions, more variants), a single backend hiccup during a render silently discards a user's in-progress pipeline; the auto-restart hides the crash, so the user just sees their work vanish with no explanation.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Single hardcoded backend/frontend port pair limits running multiple windows/instances and is fragile on shared machines (agencies, multi-seat).
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: main.js:54-58 fixed ports; single-instance lock forces one instance only.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: Agencies running per-client profiles or power users wanting parallel renders in separate windows cannot; everything funnels through one backend process and one window.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Update channel is a manual GitHub draft release with no staged rollout or rollback.
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: package.json:58-63 provider github releaseType draft; main.js:636 checkForUpdates with no channel/staging.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: As the user base grows, a bad release auto-pushes to everyone at once with no canary/rollback, and draft releases require manual promotion which is easy to get wrong.

missing_features.feature: Branded splash/loading window with real startup progress
missing_features.user_value: Eliminates the 'is it frozen?' dead time on cold start and makes the app feel native and trustworthy from second one.

missing_features.feature: Code signing for installer + auto-update (Authenticode / Trusted Signing)
missing_features.user_value: Removes the SmartScreen 'unknown publisher' wall that kills install conversion and scares non-technical buyers of a paid product.

missing_features.feature: Real license activation gate wired into startup (replace 1234/1234)
missing_features.user_value: Makes the one-time-purchase business model actually enforceable; gives buyers a proper activation/recovery flow (the LicenseService already exists, just unused).

missing_features.feature: Dynamic free-port selection for backend (and propagate to frontend API base)
missing_features.user_value: Stops EF from killing unrelated processes on 8000 and lets it start on machines where 8000 is taken — fewer mysterious launch failures.

missing_features.feature: In-app update UX: download progress, 'Check for updates' menu item, 'up to date' feedback, release notes
missing_features.user_value: Users trust and control updates instead of a silent multi-hundred-MB background download; matches CapCut/Descript update polish.

missing_features.feature: GPU/NVENC and ffmpeg preflight surfaced in UI at first run
missing_features.user_value: Sets render-speed expectations and warns about missing GPU encoding before the user waits on a slow CPU render — turns a silent fallback into informed setup.

missing_features.feature: Launch-at-login / auto-start + 'minimize to tray on startup' option
missing_features.user_value: For high-volume faceless creators running batches, the app behaves like an always-available production tool rather than something to relaunch each session.

missing_features.feature: Crash-report opt-in with bundled log upload (the crash-reporting toggle exists but only sets a flag)
missing_features.user_value: Gives the solo dev real field telemetry to fix crashes for non-technical users who can't find a log file; routes.py:357 toggle is currently inert.

missing_features.feature: Pipeline working-state persistence so backend auto-restart doesn't lose Step 2-3 work
missing_features.user_value: A backend hiccup (which the shell silently restarts) no longer wipes a user's in-progress variants mid-session — directly protects the core workflow.

missing_features.feature: macOS parity (signing/notarization, dmg, mac ffmpeg) is configured but unproven
missing_features.user_value: Opens the Mac creator market; currently only Windows is realistically shippable.

summary: This slice covers desktop first-run setup (3-step wizard: license, API keys, crash-reporting), a UI-only login gate (default 1234/1234), Lemon Squeezy license activation with a 72h offline grace period, BYOAK API-key entry, an optional ~1.5GB ML bundle installer, and multi-profile management. The plumbing is surprisingly complete and defensively coded (test-before-save, redacted hints, graceful degradation, abort handling), but it is built for a technical operator, not the stated non-technical e-commerce seller. The single biggest problem is that the onboarding wizard requires the user to paste a Supabase URL + anon key — a concept the target audience has never heard of — and there are zero "get your key here" deep links or guidance. A second serious problem is two parallel, disconnected key-storage systems: keys entered in the setup wizard (file vault + .env) never surface in the in-app per-profile key manager (Supabase vault), so an ElevenLabs key entered at onboarding does not appear in the ElevenLabs accounts panel and is not the key used by per-profile rendering. Overall maturity: solid engineering on individual pieces, but the end-to-end "install to ready" journey for a non-technical user is incomplete and confusing.
user_flow: First launch (desktop) → DesktopAuthGuard checks /desktop/auth/status → not logged in → redirect to /login → user types 1234/1234 → backend persists desktop_logged_in flag to config.json → redirect to /librarie. On the way, LicenseGuard calls /desktop/license/status; a 404 (no license) pushes the user to /setup. Setup wizard Step 1: paste Lemon Squeezy license key → /desktop/license/activate calls Lemon Squeezy, stores instance_id to license.json → auto-advance. Step 2: paste Supabase URL + anon key (required), optionally Gemini + ElevenLabs keys (or pick "Use Free TTS / Edge"), each with a Test button hitting /desktop/test-connection. Step 3: crash-reporting toggle (default off) → Finish writes keys to the file vault + AppData .env and marks first_run_complete → redirect to /librarie. Later, the user opens Settings to pick a voice, add per-profile keys (separate ApiKeyManager / ElevenLabs accounts vault), configure Postiz/Buffer, set a quota, and optionally install the ML bundle. Profile switching is a header dropdown; "Create New Profile" opens a name/description dialog.

features_present.name: 3-step setup wizard (license / keys / crash-reporting)
features_present.description: frontend/src/app/setup/page.tsx — progress bar, per-service Test buttons with idle/testing/ok/error states, Free-TTS preset, edit mode (?mode=edit) that pre-fills redacted hints, first-run guard that redirects already-activated users to /librarie.

features_present.name: Lemon Squeezy license activation + offline grace period
features_present.description: app/services/credentials/license.py — activate/validate/status against Lemon Squeezy, instance binding to hostname, 72h offline grace, 24h revalidation interval, license.json persistence. LicenseGuard re-checks every 30 min and shows a blocking 'License Expired' overlay with retry/re-key.

features_present.name: BYOAK API-key entry with validate-before-save
features_present.description: Setup wizard + /desktop/test-connection live-validate Supabase/Gemini/ElevenLabs against the real providers. ApiKeyManager (per-profile) calls /api-keys/{service}/validate and refuses to persist a key that fails auth.

features_present.name: Encrypted key vault (file-based)
features_present.description: app/services/key_vault.py — Fernet-encrypted keys.vault, machine-local salt derivation in desktop mode (decoupled from cloud creds), transparent migration of legacy SUPABASE_KEY-encrypted entries and plaintext config.json keys. Redacted ***last4 hints.

features_present.name: Per-profile multi-key vault (Supabase-backed)
features_present.description: app/api/api_key_vault_routes.py + services/credentials/vault.py — up to 3 keys/service for gemini/fal/etc, primary selection, env fallback, reveal-secret, cache invalidation on mutation. ElevenLabs has its own parallel multi-account manager with quota/tier display and failover.

features_present.name: Multi-profile management
features_present.description: profile_routes.py CRUD + set-default + dashboard; ProfileSwitcher dropdown; CreateProfileDialog. Ownership checks, CASCADE delete, can't-delete-default guard.

features_present.name: Desktop login gate
features_present.description: desktop-auth-guard.tsx + /desktop/auth/login — username/password gate, fail-closed redirect to /login, never renders protected children while unauthenticated. Explicitly documented as a UI-only test gate (1234/1234), not real API security.

features_present.name: Optional ML bundle installer
features_present.description: ml-bundle-installer.tsx — SSE-streamed download with percent/MB progress, SHA256 verify, unpack, resume, cancel/abort, 409/400/500 handling, installed-version probe. Backend probe is a documented TODO/temporary endpoint.

features_present.name: Crash reporting opt-in
features_present.description: Default-off toggle in wizard Step 3 and Settings; runtime toggle via /desktop/crash-reporting with before_send scrubbing of keys.

features_present.name: Profile activity dashboard + cost quota
features_present.description: profile_routes.py /dashboard returns project/clip/render counts and ElevenLabs/Gemini cost totals; Settings shows quota progress bar; monthly_quota_usd blocks TTS when exceeded.

features_present.name: Web auth (Supabase) login/signup/forgot-password
features_present.description: login/signup pages use Supabase Auth SDK with email confirmation, password reset, open-redirect-safe next param. Parallel path to desktop mode.

strengths.point: Validate-before-persist everywhere
strengths.why_it_matters: Both the setup wizard (/desktop/test-connection) and the per-profile ApiKeyManager hit the real provider before saving (api-key-manager.tsx:73-79). A non-technical user who pastes a wrong/expired key gets an immediate clear error instead of a silent failure three steps later in a render. This is exactly the right pattern for BYOAK.

strengths.point: Free-TTS escape hatch in onboarding
strengths.why_it_matters: setup/page.tsx:326-344 lets a user skip ElevenLabs entirely with one click (Edge TTS), so a buyer who only has a Gemini key — or none — can still reach a working app. Lowers the activation cliff for the cheapest BYOAK tier.

strengths.point: Genuine offline-first license design
strengths.why_it_matters: license.py implements a 72h grace period and machine-local vault encryption (key_vault.py:79-90) so the app keeps working without internet and the vault is not coupled to a cloud credential. This directly supports the 'offline-after-setup' differentiator versus SaaS competitors.

strengths.point: Defensive React lifecycle handling
strengths.why_it_matters: isMounted refs, AbortControllers, request-sequence guards (settings/page.tsx:479-485), and the auth guard never rendering protected children for one frame (desktop-auth-guard.tsx:58-67) prevent the class of crashes and stale-state bugs that plague long-lived desktop SPAs.

strengths.point: Transparent key migration
strengths.why_it_matters: key_vault.py:186-213 re-encrypts legacy SUPABASE_KEY-derived and plaintext config.json keys on first read, so users upgrading across the documented encryption-key change don't lose their keys or have to re-enter them — important for a paid one-time-purchase product.

ux_friction.issue: Onboarding demands a Supabase URL + anon key, which the target non-technical e-commerce seller has never heard of and cannot obtain
ux_friction.evidence: setup/page.tsx:347-390 marks Supabase URL and Anon Key as required (*) and blocks Step 2's Next button on supabaseStatus !== 'ok' (line 482). No explanation of what Supabase is, no signup link, no managed default.
ux_friction.fix_idea: For desktop one-time-purchase, ship a managed/pre-provisioned Supabase project (or local SQLite default) so the user never sees this. If self-host is required, add a 'What is this? / Create a free Supabase project' wizard with screenshots and a deep link, and pre-fill from the license server.

ux_friction.issue: No 'get your API key here' deep links or guidance for Gemini/ElevenLabs
ux_friction.evidence: setup/page.tsx:392-474 and api-key-manager.tsx:280-303 only show a password input and placeholder (AIza... / sk_...). Nothing tells a non-technical user where to create a Gemini or ElevenLabs key.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add inline 'Get a free Gemini key →' and 'Get an ElevenLabs key →' links opening the provider console, plus a one-line cost note ('~$0.22 / 1000 chars'). This is table stakes in CapCut/Creatify-style onboarding.

ux_friction.issue: Keys entered in onboarding are invisible in the in-app key manager (two disconnected vaults)
ux_friction.evidence: Setup wizard saves to the file vault + .env via /desktop/settings (platforms/desktop/routes.py:306-348, MANAGED_KEYS in key_vault.py:33-40), but Settings ApiKeyManager and ElevenLabs accounts read the Supabase-backed per-profile vault (api_key_vault_routes.py, credentials/vault.py:25). An ElevenLabs key entered at setup never appears in the ElevenLabs accounts panel and is not the per-profile key used at render.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Unify onto one vault, or at minimum seed the onboarding key into the default profile's per-profile vault and surface it in Settings so the user isn't confused about where their key 'went'.

ux_friction.issue: No voice selection during onboarding — user reaches the app unable to actually produce a video without a detour to Settings
ux_friction.evidence: setup/page.tsx never sets a voice; voice picking lives only in settings/page.tsx (loadVoices, lines 327-353). A fresh ElevenLabs user finishes setup with provider=elevenlabs but no voice_id.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add a Step 2b voice picker (or auto-select a sensible default voice) so 'Finish Setup' lands the user in a render-ready state.

ux_friction.issue: Default login credentials 1234/1234 with the placeholder literally showing '1234'
ux_friction.evidence: login/page.tsx:221 placeholder={DESKTOP_MODE ? '1234'} ; backend desktop_test_user/password default to 1234 (platforms/desktop/routes.py:206). Documented as a temporary gate.
ux_friction.fix_idea: For a shipping paid product, either remove the gate entirely (it adds friction with zero security) or replace it with a license-derived identity. At minimum stop pre-advertising the password in the placeholder.

ux_friction.issue: Profile creation captures only name/description — no per-profile keys, voice, or branding at create time
ux_friction.evidence: create-profile-dialog.tsx:71-74 posts only name+description; ProfileCreate model (profile_routes.py:23-25) has no other fields. A new profile starts empty and silently falls back to .env, so multi-brand agencies must re-walk Settings for every profile.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add an optional 'copy settings/keys from current profile' checkbox and a guided first-key step on profile creation.

ux_friction.issue: License key field has no format validation or paste-friendly UX
ux_friction.evidence: setup/page.tsx:269-278 accepts any string and only the remote activate call validates it; a mistyped key yields a generic network/activation error. No auto-trim of pasted whitespace beyond .trim(), no masked/segmented input.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Client-side validate the UUID-segmented format shown in the placeholder and surface 'looks malformed' before the round-trip.

ux_friction.issue: Settings page is a ~1700-line monolith mixing onboarding-adjacent concerns (keys, voice, quota, Postiz, Buffer, templates, ML, crash reporting)
ux_friction.evidence: settings/page.tsx is 1697 lines with ~30 useState hooks; a non-technical user faces a single very long scroll with no sectioned navigation.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Split into tabbed sections (Account, Voice/TTS, AI Keys, Publishing, Branding, Advanced) so post-setup configuration is discoverable.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: set-default profile is a two-step non-transactional update with an acknowledged race window
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: profile_routes.py:389-393 clears is_default on others then sets it on one; the comment admits 'race window exists between these calls if concurrent requests target different profiles.'
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: Concurrent default-switches (or a crash between the two writes) can leave zero or two default profiles. Low frequency on single-user desktop, but corrupts the invariant the delete-guard relies on.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Onboarding/desktop state lives in flat JSON files (config.json, license.json, keys.vault) under base_dir with no locking across the two writers
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: platforms/desktop/routes.py _read_config/_write_text rewrites the whole config.json for every flag (first_run, desktop_logged_in, crash_reporting); key_vault uses its own lock but desktop routes write .env separately.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: Concurrent writes (e.g. crash-reporting toggle during a settings save) can clobber unrelated keys via last-writer-wins on the whole-file rewrite. Fine for a single user, but fragile.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: License revalidation and key-vault decryption happen on the request path with per-call client construction
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: license.py opens a new httpx.AsyncClient per validate; LicenseGuard polls every 30 min; key_vault._get_fernet re-derives and re-reads the vault file on every get_key.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: Negligible at desktop single-user scale; would matter only if this code were reused server-side for many tenants. Noted for completeness, not a desktop concern.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Per-profile vault caps at 3 keys/service and ElevenLabs at 3 accounts
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: vault.py MAX_KEYS_PER_SERVICE=3; settings/page.tsx:1012 gates the add-account form at 3.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: High-volume faceless creators / agencies rotating many ElevenLabs accounts for character-quota will hit the ceiling and cannot scale voiceover throughput without manual key juggling.

missing_features.feature: Zero-config data backend (managed/bundled Supabase or local SQLite default) so onboarding never asks for Supabase creds
missing_features.user_value: Removes the single hardest step for a non-technical buyer; turns 'install to ready' from an impossible ask into a one-screen flow. This is the make-or-break item for the stated audience.

missing_features.feature: Provider-key acquisition guidance (deep links + screenshots + cost estimates) inside both onboarding and Settings
missing_features.user_value: Lets a Shopify seller who has never used an API actually obtain Gemini/ElevenLabs keys without leaving the app or googling. Best-in-class BYOAK tools (Creatify, n8n) all do this.

missing_features.feature: Voice selection (with preview) as part of onboarding
missing_features.user_value: User finishes setup in a render-ready state and hears their narrator immediately, rather than discovering an empty voice picker on first render attempt.

missing_features.feature: Single unified key store surfaced consistently across onboarding and Settings
missing_features.user_value: Eliminates the 'where did my key go?' confusion and the silent mismatch between the key tested at setup and the key used at render. Critical for trust in a paid product.

missing_features.feature: First-run guided tour / sample project after setup
missing_features.user_value: Shows the segment-select-once → idea → render loop on bundled sample footage so the user experiences the core value within 2 minutes, the way CapCut/Submagic onboard.

missing_features.feature: License/account portal: view plan, machine bindings, deactivate-and-move-machine, recover lost key
missing_features.user_value: A one-time-purchase buyer who reinstalls or changes PCs can self-serve instead of filing a support ticket; reduces refunds and churn.

missing_features.feature: Real authenticated identity replacing the 1234/1234 gate (or removing it)
missing_features.user_value: Either remove pointless friction or provide genuine per-user/license identity; current gate gives a false sense of security and looks unfinished in a paid app.

missing_features.feature: Onboarding completeness checklist / health panel ('Supabase ✓, Gemini ✓, Voice ✗, ML bundle optional')
missing_features.user_value: Gives a non-technical user a single at-a-glance 'am I ready?' status and one-click jumps to fix each gap, instead of discovering missing config mid-workflow.

missing_features.feature: Import/export of profile config and keys between machines
missing_features.user_value: Agencies managing many client profiles can migrate or back up setup without re-entering every key and brand color per machine.

missing_features.user_value: Agencies spinning up a new client profile set it up once in the create dialog rather than re-walking the entire 1700-line Settings page.

summary: The 4-step pipeline (idea -> N script variants -> TTS -> preview/match -> render) is the most mature, deeply-built subsystem in the product and largely delivers on the "type an idea, get finished videos" promise. It supports multi-variant generation, per-variant regenerate loops for both scripts and voiceover, a real timeline editor with keyword segment matching, meta-multiplication (2x visually-distinct variants for Instagram/Facebook), inline subtitle styling, interstitial slides, PiP overlays, and batch render with a render-skip cache. Contrary to the stated pain point, pipeline state is now persisted to editai_pipelines at every step (_db_save_pipeline is called after generate/TTS/preview/matches edits) and restored via _get_pipeline_or_load + the frontend ?id= URL restore, so a backend restart does NOT lose Step 2-3 work — only ephemeral progress percentages are lost. The two genuinely unsolved weaknesses are: (1) preview is a 30-300s server-side FFmpeg file render rather than in-player compositing, and (2) the whole experience lives in a single 3698-line page.tsx monolith plus a 6502-line router, which is a velocity/maintenance bottleneck more than a user-facing one. A parallel "Simple Mode" (simple-mode-pipeline.tsx) offers a 3-click preset->upload->download flow but is shallow and re-uploads a fresh video each run.
user_flow: Advanced mode: user lands on /pipeline, types an idea + optional brand context, optionally attaches catalog products (which filters segment keywords to those product groups), picks variant count (1-10), target duration, and provider (Gemini/Claude), then clicks Generate. Backend pulls the profile's segment keywords from editai_segments, builds a duration-aware prompt, and returns N TTS-safe scripts; UI advances to Step 2. In Step 2 the user reviews/edits each script inline (DebouncedTextarea), can regenerate one or all scripts, sees live ElevenLabs credit balance, then generates TTS per-variant or "Regenerate All TTS"; Whisper produces SRT timing. In Step 3 each variant's SRT phrases are keyword-matched to segments and shown in a TimelineEditor where the user can swap/reorder/merge segments, tweak subtitle style, add interstitial slides and PiP overlays, then click "Preview" which fires a server-side FFmpeg render (540x960 ultrafast) and polls preview-status until an MP4 is ready to play in a dialog. Timeline edits auto-save (debounced PUT /matches). In Step 4 the user selects variants, a check-render call detects which can be skipped (cache hit), and a batch render kicks off background FFmpeg jobs polled via status. Simple mode: pick a style preset, drag a video, click Generate — it creates a project, uploads the video, auto-generates scripts, and renders all variants, then offers Download/Download All.

features_present.name: Multi-variant script generation
features_present.description: Gemini/Claude generate 1-10 variants with duration-aware word targets, product-group keyword awareness, per-profile AI instructions, and 4-strategy parse fallback + TTS sanitization (app/services/script_generator.py:52, app/api/pipeline_routes.py:2524).

features_present.name: Per-variant + bulk regenerate loops
features_present.description: Regenerate one script, all scripts (with AbortController cancel), one TTS, or all TTS without leaving Step 2/3 (page.tsx:2832,2865,2921,2988,3085).

features_present.name: Pipeline state persistence + restore
features_present.description: Full pipeline (scripts, tts_previews, previews, preview_renders, matches, subtitle overrides) upserted to editai_pipelines at every mutation and reloaded via _get_pipeline_or_load; frontend restores from ?id= URL and DB on mount, including stale-temp-audio detection (pipeline_routes.py:707,1267,1371; page.tsx:548).

features_present.name: Keyword segment matching + timeline editor
features_present.description: SRT phrases auto-matched to segments by keyword; user can edit/reorder/merge/swap in TimelineEditor with debounced persistence (page.tsx:480-516).

features_present.name: Server-side preview render with fingerprint cache
features_present.description: Fast 540x960 CRF32 preview with a sha256 fingerprint over matches+TTS-mtime+slides+filters; cache hit returns instantly, per-preview asyncio lock prevents races (pipeline_routes.py:5564).

features_present.name: Meta-multiplication (2x distinct variants)
features_present.description: Generates Instagram + Facebook visually-distinct versions per variant to dodge duplicate-content penalties, with per-version subtitle style overrides (pipeline_routes.py:5591, _resolve_meta_preview_variant).

features_present.name: Batch render with skip-cache
features_present.description: check-render detects already-rendered variants so re-renders skip unchanged work; renders run as background FFmpeg jobs under a concurrency semaphore (pipeline_routes.py:3397,3573; page.tsx:1669).

features_present.name: Style presets
features_present.description: 5 backend presets (energetic/showcase/calm/demo/cinematic) mapping to voice + pacing + subtitle params, mirrored in frontend types (app/services/pipeline_presets.py:13).

features_present.name: Simple Mode (3-click flow)
features_present.description: Preset -> upload -> generate -> download-all, with status polling and a 'switch to advanced' escape hatch on failure (simple-mode-pipeline.tsx).

features_present.name: Subtitle styling / interstitials / PiP / video filters
features_present.description: Per-variant subtitle settings, interstitial title slides, picture-in-picture overlays, and denoise/sharpen/color filters threaded through preview and render (pipeline_routes.py:5611-5647).

features_present.name: Manual script authoring path
features_present.description: handleCreateManual seeds empty script slots via /pipeline/import so users can write scripts by hand without AI (page.tsx:1348).

features_present.name: In-pipeline caption generator + scheduling
features_present.description: Two large embedded components (pipeline-caption-generator.tsx ~645 lines, pipeline-schedule.tsx ~644 lines) extend the pipeline into captions and publish scheduling.

strengths.point: State durability is already solved, despite the brief's assumption — every step writes to editai_pipelines and restores cleanly
strengths.why_it_matters: The single scariest failure for a bulk-production tool (lose an hour of editing on a crash) is largely averted; this is a real moat over a naive in-memory competitor and should be marketed, not feared.

strengths.point: The fingerprint preview cache is genuinely clever — it keys on TTS mtime + per-segment match payload + filters, so unchanged previews return instantly
strengths.why_it_matters: Bulk creators re-preview constantly; instant cache hits on unchanged variants are the difference between usable and painful at 10+ variants.

strengths.point: Script generation is hardened far beyond a demo: 4 parse-fallback strategies, TTS sanitization, safety-filter detection, retry-on-429/5xx, duration-aware word targets, per-profile keys
strengths.why_it_matters: Non-technical sellers will paste messy ideas in any language; robust parsing/sanitization is what keeps the 'just works' promise from breaking on edge cases competitors stumble on.

strengths.point: Meta-multiplication is a real differentiator — auto-producing 2 visually-distinct cuts per script to dodge Meta duplicate-content penalties
strengths.why_it_matters: No mainstream SaaS (OpusClip/Submagic) ships this; for high-volume faceless creators posting to IG+FB it's a concrete reason to buy.

strengths.point: Deterministic, local, zero-per-video FFmpeg assembly with a concurrency semaphore
strengths.why_it_matters: Delivers the core BYOAK value prop — render 50 videos for the cost of 50 TTS calls, no upload, no per-export fee — which SaaS structurally cannot match.

ux_friction.issue: Preview is a 30-300s server-side file render, not in-player compositing; any single-segment edit changes the fingerprint and forces a full re-render
ux_friction.evidence: pipeline_routes.py:5611-5654 builds the fingerprint over every match's segment_id/start/end/transforms; one swap invalidates it and triggers assemble_and_render_preview with a 300s timeout (5736-5765)
ux_friction.fix_idea: Move to client-side preview: play the actual segment source files in sequence with WebVTT subtitle overlay and the TTS audio track in an HTML5/canvas player, reserving the FFmpeg render only for final export. At minimum, render previews incrementally/segment-diffed so a 1-clip edit re-renders 1 clip, not the whole timeline.

ux_friction.issue: No visible cross-variant progress or ETA during batch render/preview — only per-variant percentages, and progress is in-memory so a refresh shows blanks
ux_friction.evidence: _generation_progress/preview_renders progress is ephemeral; simple-mode overallProgress averages only variants already returned (simple-mode-pipeline.tsx:281), and restored pipelines lose live progress (pipeline_routes.py:1371 restores paths but not progress)
ux_friction.fix_idea: Show an aggregate 'Rendering 3 of 8, ~2m left' bar driven by a persisted job table (jobs already exists) so progress survives refresh/restart and feels like a batch tool.

ux_friction.issue: Simple Mode re-uploads a fresh video and auto-generates a generic preset prompt every run, ignoring the creator's pre-selected segment library
ux_friction.evidence: simple-mode-pipeline.tsx:191-207 uploads uploadedFile to a new project then calls /pipeline/generate with presetToPrompt(preset) — a canned string, not the user's idea or their existing segment library
ux_friction.fix_idea: Make Simple Mode operate on the existing segment library + an idea box (the product's actual value prop is 'select segments once, mass-produce'); the upload-per-run model contradicts the vision and confuses non-technical users.

ux_friction.issue: Render proceeds on un-previewed variants behind only a window.confirm warning, risking unexpected cuts
ux_friction.evidence: page.tsx:1677-1683 uses a native confirm() when a selected variant has no matches; if dismissed-through, match_overrides are missing and the render auto-matches differently than the user will expect
ux_friction.fix_idea: Auto-preview (or auto-match server-side) any selected-but-unpreviewed variant before render, or block render until all selected variants have a confirmed timeline; replace native confirm with an inline checklist.

ux_friction.issue: Everything is gated behind AI key setup with no template/example-driven first run
ux_friction.evidence: generate_pipeline hard-requires gemini/anthropic keys (script_generator.py:489-494) and there's no sample-idea or one-click demo path; a buyer with no key configured hits an error wall
ux_friction.fix_idea: Ship a 'Try with sample idea' + bundled example segments so a new non-technical buyer reaches a finished video before configuring BYOAK, then prompt for keys.

ux_friction.issue: 4-step linear wizard with no global 'what changed / needs re-render' indicator across variants
ux_friction.evidence: Step state is a single integer (page.tsx:94) and staleness is tracked per-TTS only (restoredTts stale flag, page.tsx:585); editing a script in Step 2 doesn't visibly flag its Step 3 preview as stale
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add per-variant status chips (script edited / TTS stale / preview stale / rendered) visible across all steps so bulk users know exactly which of 10 variants need attention.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: All pipeline working state lives in a process-global _pipelines dict capped at 1000 entries with oldest-first eviction
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: _MAX_PIPELINE_ENTRIES = 1000 (pipeline_routes.py:273); _evict_old_pipelines drops oldest by created_at (528-548). DB-backed reload mitigates correctness, but an evicted active pipeline silently reloads mid-session
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: For a single desktop user this is fine; if ever multi-tenant/cloud, 1000 concurrent pipelines across users will thrash the cache and every evicted-then-touched pipeline pays a full DB round-trip + disk-existence checks on each access (1271-1371).

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Preview and render are server-side FFmpeg jobs serialized by a global semaphore on one machine
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: acquire_preview_slot()/acquire_render_slot() gate all FFmpeg; render-preview holds a per-preview asyncio lock and 300s timeout (pipeline_routes.py:5729,5765); CLAUDE.md notes a single FFmpeg concurrency pool
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: At 10x volume (a seller batching 50 SKUs x 3 variants x 2 meta-versions = 300 renders) jobs queue head-of-line on one CPU/GPU; there is no distributed queue, no priority, and a desktop user's UI blocks behind the semaphore. This is the hard ceiling on 'mass production'.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: page.tsx is a 3698-line monolith and pipeline_routes.py is 6502 lines holding 40+ endpoints
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: wc -l: page.tsx 3698, pipeline_routes.py 6502; the page holds ~60+ useState hooks for all 4 steps (page.tsx:94-219)
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: Not user-facing, but it caps iteration speed and makes regressions likely as features (captions, scheduling, meta) keep accreting into the same files; every new variant feature risks a 60-state re-render storm.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Per-variant TTS is generated sequentially per request with no batching/queue, and full DB upsert of the entire pipeline blob on every mutation
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: _db_save_pipeline serializes previews+render_jobs+tts_previews+preview_renders+captions JSON on every step (pipeline_routes.py:723-761); called ~15 distinct places
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: At high variant counts the whole pipeline JSON (which grows with previews/captions/overrides) is rewritten on each small edit, so a 10-variant pipeline with captions does large redundant upserts; timeline auto-save (debounced 800ms) compounds this under active editing.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: ElevenLabs character cost scales linearly with no pre-render cost estimate or guardrail in the pipeline UI
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: Cost noted at ~$0.22/1000 chars (CLAUDE.md); Step 2 shows live credit balance (page.tsx:154) but there's no per-batch projected-cost preview before 'Regenerate All TTS' across N variants
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: A seller batching hundreds of scripts can burn their BYOAK ElevenLabs credits unexpectedly; at 10x this becomes the dominant variable cost with no visibility before the spend.

missing_features.feature: In-player (client-side) preview instead of server FFmpeg file render
missing_features.user_value: Instant, scrubbable preview of timeline + subtitles + voiceover so edits feel real-time like CapCut/Descript, eliminating the 30-300s wait that kills the edit loop

missing_features.feature: True bulk/batch pipeline: one idea (or a CSV/product-feed of ideas) -> dozens of finished videos in one queue with an aggregate dashboard
missing_features.user_value: This IS the stated vision ('mass production'); today the flow is one-idea-at-a-time per pipeline. A seller with 50 SKUs needs a queue, not 50 manual wizard runs

missing_features.feature: Pre-render cost + duration estimate ('this batch will use ~12,400 ElevenLabs chars / ~$2.70 and ~8 min')
missing_features.user_value: BYOAK users need spend visibility before committing a batch; prevents bill shock and builds trust in the zero-per-video pitch

missing_features.feature: Per-variant staleness/status indicators visible across all 4 steps
missing_features.user_value: When juggling 10 variants, users instantly see which need re-TTS or re-preview; removes guesswork and accidental stale renders

missing_features.feature: Hook/CTA/template library + brand-voice presets that steer the AI prompt
missing_features.user_value: Best-in-class (Submagic/OpusClip) auto-apply viral hooks and consistent brand voice; sellers want repeatable on-brand output, not a blank idea box

missing_features.feature: Resume/'continue where you left off' surfaced prominently (state is already persisted)
missing_features.user_value: The persistence exists but is only reachable via ?id= URL; a visible 'Recent pipelines / resume' rail turns a hidden strength into a loved feature

missing_features.feature: Sample-idea / demo-without-keys onboarding path
missing_features.user_value: Lets a non-technical buyer reach a finished video before BYOAK setup, dramatically improving activation/refund rates

missing_features.feature: A/B-oriented multi-variant output (auto-label hook style per variant, suggest best performer)
missing_features.user_value: Variant generation exists but variants aren't differentiated by strategy; labeling 'curiosity hook / problem-solution / social-proof' makes the multi-variant feature purposeful for testing

missing_features.feature: Auto-music / trending-audio bed with ducking under voiceover
missing_features.user_value: Every competitor ships background music; its absence makes outputs feel flat versus TikTok-native content

missing_features.feature: Distributed/queued render backend (or at least a persisted job queue with priority) decoupled from the request lifecycle
missing_features.user_value: Lets large batches run reliably overnight and survive restarts without head-of-line blocking the UI; required to honor 'mass production' at scale

summary: This slice covers getting a creator's own footage in, marking reusable segments with keywords, and picking those segments for a generated script. Ingestion is genuinely good for a desktop app: drag-and-drop resolves the real local path (no upload/copy), a native file picker exists, and metadata/thumbnail/preview-proxy run in the background. The "select once, reuse forever" library exists with usage_count, favorites, single-use flags, notes, and per-segment visual transforms. The big gaps are in intelligence and transparency: tagging is 100% manual (the GeminiVideoAnalyzer that could auto-suggest tags/segments is built but only wired into the legacy library pipeline, never into this page), and keyword-to-script matching is a naive case-insensitive substring check with no synonyms, no semantics, and an invisible round-robin fallback the user can't see or steer. Overall maturity is solid-but-mechanical: the plumbing is mature, the "AI does the boring part" promise is largely unfulfilled in this slice.
user_flow: 1) User opens the Segments page and adds source footage by dragging a file in (frontend/src/app/segments/page.tsx:519 handleDrop → POST /segments/find-local locates it on disk by name+size and registers the path without uploading), or via native picker (GET /segments/browse-local), or manual upload fallback. 2) Background task extracts metadata + thumbnail + preview proxy (segments_routes.py:823). 3) User selects a video, scrubs the timeline in video-segment-player.tsx (which overlays a waveform and Silero voice-detection regions to help find speech), and drags to mark a start/end. 4) A popup (simple-segment-popup.tsx) asks for keywords (manually typed, with comma/semicolon paste and 7 generic quick-add chips), optional notes, and a "use once" toggle. 5) The segment is saved (POST /source-videos/{id}/segments) into a profile-scoped library shown in a "current video" or "all segments" grid (capped at 500, client-side filtered by keyword/favorite/search). 6) Later, in the pipeline, the generated script's SRT phrases are matched to these segments by substring keyword match with round-robin auto-fill (assembly_service.py:503 match_srt_to_segments) to assemble the video. There is no step where AI proposes segments or tags — the user does all marking and labeling by hand.

features_present.name: No-upload local ingestion (drag-drop + path resolution)
features_present.description: Dragging a file resolves its real disk path via /find-local (name+size search across user dirs then drives) and registers it in place; /source-videos/local stores the path with no copy/transcode. Native picker (/browse-local) and a traditional upload fallback also exist. Ideal for a privacy/local-first desktop app.

features_present.name: Background metadata + thumbnail + preview proxy
features_present.description: _process_local_video_background extracts duration/dimensions/fps, generates a thumbnail, and builds a preview proxy without transcoding the original. Frontend polls source-video status until ready.

features_present.name: Waveform + voice-activity overlay on the timeline
features_present.description: video-segment-player.tsx fetches a downsampled waveform and Silero VAD voice regions and renders them on the scrub timeline, so a user can visually find where speech happens before cutting a segment.

features_present.name: Manual segment marking + keyword popup
features_present.description: Drag-to-select start/end then simple-segment-popup.tsx captures keywords (Enter/comma/semicolon, paste-split), notes, and single-use. clip-tag-editor.tsx provides a separate tag-chip editor (max 20).

features_present.name: Reusable segment library with usage signals
features_present.description: Profile-scoped library with usage_count, is_favorite, single_use, notes, product_group, and per-segment transforms. Library view supports keyword/favorite/duration/search filters and reset-usage.

features_present.name: Per-segment visual transforms
features_present.description: segment_transforms.py builds FFmpeg filter chains for rotation/scale/pan/flip/opacity, with segment-default vs project-override merge. Clean, deterministic, identity-short-circuited.

features_present.name: Per-segment extraction cache
features_present.description: segment_cache.py is a content-addressed (source path+mtime+size+cut+transforms+codec) LRU cache so an iterative edit re-extracts only touched segments. Crash-safe atomic writes, GB cap eviction.

features_present.name: Keyword-to-SRT matching with round-robin fallback
features_present.description: assembly_service.match_srt_to_segments does substring keyword matching, then a sophisticated round-robin auto-fill with cooldown, source-video interleaving, overlap avoidance, single-use tracking, and product-group scoping.

features_present.name: Fuzzy keyword matcher (keyword_matcher.py)
features_present.description: A separate matcher with diacritics normalization, prefix/suffix/fuzzy SequenceMatcher scoring and dedup — but it is used only for the legacy secondary-video flow (video_processor.py), NOT for the main pipeline matching, which uses plain substring.

features_present.name: Gemini video analyzer
features_present.description: GeminiVideoAnalyzer scores frames and returns scored/tagged best-moment segments, with batch offset handling and cost logging. Fully built but only wired into the legacy video_processor library flow, never surfaced in the Segments tagging UI.

features_present.name: Product-group segmentation
features_present.description: Segments can be grouped (product_group) so matching can scope to a product; group CRUD + reassign endpoints exist. Supports the e-commerce multi-SKU audience.

strengths.point: True local-first ingestion with zero upload
strengths.why_it_matters: The /find-local path-resolution + /source-videos/local in-place registration is the concrete delivery of the privacy/no-upload differentiator vs OpusClip/HeyGen. For a 50-500 SKU seller with gigabytes of footage, not copying or uploading is a massive UX and cost win.

strengths.point: Waveform + Silero voice-activity overlay during segment selection
strengths.why_it_matters: This is genuinely best-in-class assist for manual cutting — competitors charge for VAD-based silence detection. It directly reduces the effort of the once-only marking step the whole product depends on.

strengths.point: The round-robin matcher is unusually thoughtful
strengths.why_it_matters: Cooldown-based reuse spacing, source-video interleaving, overlap-time avoidance, single-use tracking and per-variant offset (assembly_service.py:503-880) mean generated videos don't repeat the same clip back-to-back. This is the deterministic 'everything-else' engine the vision promises, and it works.

strengths.point: Content-addressed segment cache
strengths.why_it_matters: segment_cache.py keys on everything that changes the output bytes, so a one-segment edit doesn't re-extract the whole timeline — the right architecture to eventually fix the slow-preview pain point in this slice's territory.

ux_friction.issue: Tagging is 100% manual — no AI tag/segment suggestions despite an analyzer existing
ux_friction.evidence: simple-segment-popup.tsx:229 offers only 7 hardcoded generic chips (intro/product/demo/...); grep for 'analyze|gemini|suggest|transcri' in frontend/src/app/segments/page.tsx returns NO matches; GeminiVideoAnalyzer (gemini_analyzer.py) is only referenced from video_processor.py, never this page.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add an 'Auto-tag / Suggest segments' button that runs GeminiVideoAnalyzer (or a local transcript) on the source video and pre-populates candidate segments with keywords the user can accept/edit. This is the single biggest lever to make 'select once' feel magical instead of tedious.

ux_friction.issue: Keyword matching is naive substring with no synonyms/lemmas/semantics
ux_friction.evidence: assembly_service.py:782 `if keyword_lower in srt_text_lower` and confidence is a flat 1.0/0.7 (line 785). The diacritics-aware fuzzy matcher in keyword_matcher.py is NOT used here. A keyword 'shoe' won't match script word 'sneakers'/'footwear'; 'run' substring-matches 'running' but also 'runway'.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Route the main pipeline through keyword_matcher (fuzzy + diacritics) and add a lightweight synonym/embedding layer so the user doesn't have to brainstorm every surface form of a word. Show match confidence in the timeline.

ux_friction.issue: Match results are opaque — user can't see why a clip was chosen or that round-robin filled a gap
ux_friction.evidence: match_srt_to_segments returns MatchResult with is_auto/keyword but the segments page has no match-preview; the user only discovers a bad pick after a 30-80s render. Round-robin auto-fill (assembly_service.py:854) is invisible.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Surface a per-phrase match panel ('matched on keyword X' vs 'auto-filled, no keyword') with the chosen clip thumbnail and a one-click swap, before rendering.

ux_friction.issue: Generic quick-add chips don't reflect the user's own footage or product vocabulary
ux_friction.evidence: simple-segment-popup.tsx:229 list is hardcoded; ClipTagEditor has no autocomplete from existing tags.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Populate quick-add and an autocomplete dropdown from the profile's most-used existing keywords so tagging stays consistent across a 500-SKU library.

ux_friction.issue: Library view is a flat 500-cap grid with client-side filtering only
ux_friction.evidence: page.tsx:288 fetches '/segments/?limit=500' once; filteredSegments (page.tsx:301) filters in JS. No pagination UI, no server-side keyword search beyond a single eq filter.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add server-side paginated search + facet counts by keyword/source video; show 'used in N videos' and 'last used' so creators can curate their reusable bank.

ux_friction.issue: No bulk segment operations or scene auto-split
ux_friction.evidence: Segments are created one drag at a time via the popup; no 'auto-split into scenes' or multi-select tag-apply in page.tsx handlers.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add FFmpeg scene-detection (or reuse VAD) to propose a full set of candidate cuts in one pass, plus multi-select to apply a keyword to many segments at once.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: N+1 source-video name lookups on every segment list
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: segments_routes.py:1477-1481 loops unique_video_ids calling repo.get_source_video(vid) per id; same pattern in get_project_segments (2415+). With Supabase this is one round-trip per distinct source video per list call.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: At 10x footage (hundreds of source videos), the library list and project-segment composition incur hundreds of serial Supabase calls, making the Segments page and pipeline assembly noticeably slow.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Whole library loaded and filtered client-side, capped at 500
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: page.tsx:288 '/segments/?limit=500' with no pagination; filteredSegments filters in JS (page.tsx:301).
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: A serious creator with thousands of reusable segments silently loses everything past 500 (they simply won't appear for matching/curation), and the browser holds/filters the full array on every keystroke.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Matching is O(srt_entries * segments * keywords) substring scan per variant
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: assembly_service.py:771-793 nested loops over search_segments x keywords for each SRT entry, repeated per variant_index.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: With many variants and a large segment bank this grows quadratically; fine at MVP scale but will need an inverted keyword index if libraries reach thousands of segments.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: /find-local can walk entire drives
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: segments_routes.py:638-666 falls back to os.walk over every drive (depth 6) when a file isn't in common dirs.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: On a machine with large external drives a single mis-resolved drop can stall the request for many seconds; acceptable now but should be cancellable/time-boxed.

missing_features.feature: AI auto-segment + auto-tag from the source video
missing_features.user_value: Turns the tedious once-only marking into 'review and accept' — the analyzer already exists, it just needs a button on the Segments page that proposes cuts with keywords. This is the difference between a labeling chore and a delightful 'AI did it for me' moment.

missing_features.feature: Transcript/speech-based segment suggestion
missing_features.user_value: For talking-head footage, auto-cut on sentences (reuse the existing VAD/Whisper timing) and seed keywords from the spoken words, so segments align to natural beats.

missing_features.feature: Semantic / synonym-aware matching with visible confidence
missing_features.user_value: A creator shouldn't have to list every word form; 'sneaker' should match 'shoe/footwear'. Showing confidence lets them trust the auto-assembly instead of re-rendering blindly.

missing_features.feature: Pre-render match preview with one-click swap
missing_features.user_value: See which clip each script line got (keyword vs auto-fill) and swap before paying the 30-80s render cost — removes the worst loop in the whole product for this slice.

missing_features.feature: Keyword autocomplete from existing library + tag taxonomy
missing_features.user_value: Keeps tags consistent across hundreds of SKUs so matching actually fires; prevents the 'shoes' vs 'shoe' vs 'sneaker' fragmentation that silently breaks matching.

missing_features.feature: Bulk multi-select tag/delete/favorite + scene auto-split
missing_features.user_value: Lets a high-volume creator tag dozens of segments at once and turn a long source video into candidate cuts in one pass instead of dragging each manually.

missing_features.feature: Reuse insight: 'used in N videos / last used / never used'
missing_features.user_value: Helps creators curate their reusable bank, find dead weight, and trust the 'reuse forever' promise; surfaces underused great clips.

missing_features.feature: Server-side paginated/faceted segment search
missing_features.user_value: Makes the library usable past 500 segments and fast at 10x scale, which the target 50-500 SKU audience will hit quickly.

summary: This slice covers two parallel preview systems plus the manual editing surface. (1) A genuinely impressive "Instant Preview" in timeline-editor.tsx that composites source segments client-side by pooling muted <video> elements per source-video-id and driving them off a master audio clock via requestAnimationFrame (frontend/src/components/timeline-editor.tsx:327-353, 255-323). This sidesteps the 30-80s render-file latency entirely for the common case. (2) A server-file preview (variant-preview-player.tsx) that POSTs match overrides, polls/SSEs a fingerprinted FFmpeg render at 540x960, and plays the resulting MP4 — this is the slow path that still owns final-fidelity review (transforms, PiP, color, denoise). Editing power is modest: segment swap, drag-to-swap-assignment, +/-0.5s duration nudges, merge-group-aware grouping, and interstitial image slides with Ken Burns. The maturity is high on playback engineering (lots of careful seek/grace/merge-group handling) but the editor itself is a "segment-mapping" tool, not a real NLE timeline — there is no trim-within-segment, no ripple, no multi-track, no per-segment transition picker, and transforms/PiP/logo are batch-config dialogs rather than direct on-canvas manipulation. Against CapCut/Descript/Submagic this is a constrained, opinionated bulk-production editor, not a free-form editor — which is on-brand, but several table-stakes editing affordances are missing.

features_present.name: Instant client-side composite preview
features_present.description: Plays source segments directly in pooled muted <video> elements synced to a master audio clock via rAF (~60fps segment switching), with merge-group awareness, seek-grace windows, segment-end enforcement, and next-segment preload. No render wait. timeline-editor.tsx:327-505, 552-702.

features_present.name: Server-side render preview (final-fidelity)
features_present.description: POST /pipeline/render-preview with full match overrides, fingerprint cache, SSE progress + polling fallback, 540x960 output, limitations banner. variant-preview-player.tsx:114-291; pipeline_routes.py:5564-5797.

features_present.name: Timeline + List dual view
features_present.description: Horizontal proportional-width strip grouped by merge_group, plus a vertical list with drag handles, status badges, confidence, and product-group tags. timeline-editor.tsx:1417-2116.

features_present.name: Segment swap / assign dialog
features_present.description: Keyword search, same-source vs all-source filter, proximity +/-2 exclusion to avoid repeats, propagates swap to whole merge group. timeline-editor.tsx:704-821, 2118-2229.

features_present.name: Drag-to-swap assignment
features_present.description: Native HTML5 DnD swaps the segment mapping between two positions (SRT text/timing stays put). timeline-editor.tsx:825-897.

features_present.name: Duration override per phrase
features_present.description: +/-0.5s nudge buttons clamped 0.5-10s; shows adjusted vs natural SRT duration. timeline-editor.tsx:901-909, 1948-1993. No drag-resize of the block itself.

features_present.name: Interstitial image slides
features_present.description: Insert product-image slides between blocks with duration 0.5-5s and Ken Burns directions. URL-only input, no upload/picker. timeline-editor.tsx:911-943, 1444-1743.

features_present.name: Global + per-segment transforms
features_present.description: Rotation/scale/pan/flip/opacity via sliders with click-to-edit values; global panel offers set-vs-add modes. CSS-preview only in segment player; not visible in the instant timeline preview. global-transform-panel.tsx, segment-transform-panel.tsx.

features_present.name: PiP overlay config
features_present.description: Position (4 corners), size (S/M/L), animation (static/fade/kenburns) presets. Discrete choices only — no free placement or sizing. pip-overlay-panel.tsx.

features_present.name: Logo drag overlay
features_present.description: Drag + scale logo over a still image with quick-position presets and real-coordinate mapping. Works on generated images, NOT on the video timeline. logo-drag-overlay.tsx.

features_present.name: Source video segment marker (creation surface)
features_present.description: Rich pro-grade marking tool: waveform, voice-region detection overlay, zoomable timeline, frame-step, J/K/L speed, mark/group hotkeys, segment resize handles, fullscreen. video-segment-player.tsx.

features_present.name: Subtitle overlay in instant preview
features_present.description: Live CSS-rendered subtitle honoring fontSize/outline/shadow/glow/opacity/positionY scaled from ASS PlayRes 1920. timeline-editor.tsx:1142-1187.

user_flow: A user reaches Step 3 (Preview & Match) after TTS. The timeline auto-fills each SRT phrase with a keyword-matched segment (green=matched, amber=unmatched, grey=auto-filled). The user scans the horizontal strip, clicks a block to open an inline mini-player (a single seeking <video>) plus details/swap/duration controls, or switches to List view to drag handles and nudge durations. To fix a bad match they open the swap dialog, search by keyword, optionally filter to the same source, and pick a replacement (which propagates to the whole merge group). To sanity-check the whole video without a render they click "Instant Preview": pooled videos and the TTS audio start, and blocks highlight as playback advances; they can scrub, skip segment-to-segment, click a block to seek, or expand to a larger dialog. For final-fidelity review (transforms, PiP, color, denoise baked in) they open the VariantPreviewPlayer, which triggers a server FFmpeg render, shows a progress bar for 30-80s, then plays a 540x960 MP4 with a "resolution/loudness differ from export" note. Transforms, PiP, and logo are configured in separate side panels/dialogs, not by manipulating the preview canvas directly.

strengths.point: Instant in-browser composite preview is a genuine differentiator
strengths.why_it_matters: It eliminates the 30-80s render wait for the most common review action (does the timeline look right?). The pooled-video + master-audio-clock + rAF architecture (timeline-editor.tsx:327-353) is what OpusClip/Submagic users wish they had — most SaaS editors force a server round-trip per edit. For bulk producers iterating on dozens of videos, this is the single biggest UX win in the product.

strengths.point: Playback engineering is unusually careful
strengths.why_it_matters: Seek-grace windows, merge-group transition detection, segment-end enforcement at 60fps instead of 4Hz timeupdate, activation-id invalidation to kill stale async work, and per-source preload hints (timeline-editor.tsx:236-241, 401-449, 552-671) show real attention to the subtle stalls/black-frames that plague homemade players. This is the hard part and it is done well.

strengths.point: Fingerprint-cached server preview avoids redundant renders
strengths.why_it_matters: The sha256 fingerprint over matches + visual settings + audio mtime (pipeline_routes.py:5650-5667) returns a cache hit instantly when nothing changed, and the per-pipeline preview lock prevents concurrent FFmpeg races. This keeps the slow path from being slower than it needs to be.

strengths.point: Merge-group-aware editing keeps preview and render in agreement
strengths.why_it_matters: Swaps propagate to all entries in a merge group (timeline-editor.tsx:790-821) because the render collapses a group to its first segment. Getting this right means the instant preview actually matches the exported video — a correctness property many quick-preview hacks get wrong.

strengths.point: The source-video marking tool (video-segment-player.tsx) is pro-grade
strengths.why_it_matters: Waveform, voice-activity overlay, zoomable timeline with wheel zoom, frame-stepping, J/K/L, I/O/C/G hotkeys, and drag-resize handles rival a real NLE's clip view. This makes the 'select important segments once' core promise credible.

ux_friction.issue: Two separate preview systems with different fidelity confuse 'what will I actually get'
ux_friction.evidence: Instant Preview (timeline-editor.tsx:1056-1093) shows NO transforms, PiP, logo, color, denoise, interstitial Ken Burns, or transitions — it just plays raw source segments + CSS subtitles. The accurate preview is the 30-80s server render (variant-preview-player.tsx). A user who tweaks rotation/PiP and watches Instant Preview sees none of it and may ship wrong.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Render transforms/PiP/logo via CSS in the instant player (the segment player already does CSS transforms at video-segment-player.tsx:1024-1045 — reuse it), and add a clear badge: 'Instant preview = layout only; click Full Preview for final look.'

ux_friction.issue: No trim-within-segment in the editor
ux_friction.evidence: Duration is only adjustable via +/-0.5s buttons clamped to 0.5-10s (timeline-editor.tsx:901-909); there is no way to choose WHICH 2s of a 5s segment plays. segment_start_time/end_time come from matching and are not user-editable here.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add drag handles on the timeline block (the source player already implements resize at video-segment-player.tsx:811-878 — port the interaction) so users can slide the in/out point within the matched segment.

ux_friction.issue: Transforms / PiP / logo are blind batch dialogs, not direct manipulation
ux_friction.evidence: global-transform-panel.tsx and pip-overlay-panel.tsx are slider/preset panels with no live preview of the result on the actual video; logo-drag-overlay.tsx only works over a still image, not the video. PiP is locked to 4 corners + 3 sizes (pip-overlay-panel.tsx:20-31).
ux_friction.fix_idea: Composite transforms/PiP/logo onto the instant-preview canvas with draggable handles (CapCut/Veed standard). At minimum, free X/Y/size for PiP instead of corner presets.

ux_friction.issue: Interstitial slide image is a raw URL text field with no upload or picker
ux_friction.evidence: timeline-editor.tsx:1651-1659 — user must paste 'https://...'; non-technical e-commerce sellers cannot easily get a URL for a local product photo.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add a file-upload / product-image picker (a product-picker-dialog already exists per the PiP import) and let users drop a local image.

ux_friction.issue: Instant preview is gated behind a click and rebuilds state each time
ux_friction.evidence: canPreview requires pipelineId+profileId+>=1 video match; audio preloads but activation runs a multi-stage rAF/canplay/seek dance (timeline-editor.tsx:552-671) that can take a moment on first play, and there is no scrub-thumbnail or frame preview before pressing play.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Auto-activate a paused first frame so the user sees the opening shot immediately, and show segment thumbnails on the scrub bar.

ux_friction.issue: No transition picker despite 'transitions' being a charter feature
ux_friction.evidence: No transition UI anywhere in this slice; blocks are hard cuts. The pipeline mentions transitions but the editor exposes no per-cut control.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add a between-block transition affordance (the '+' insert pattern at timeline-editor.tsx:1429-1441 is a natural host) with crossfade/whip/slide presets.

ux_friction.issue: Drag-to-swap semantics are non-obvious
ux_friction.evidence: Dragging a block swaps segment ASSIGNMENTS but leaves SRT text/timing in place (timeline-editor.tsx:855-889); the tooltip says 'Drag to swap segment assignment' (line 1923) but users expect reordering clips, not swapping mappings.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Clarify with a drag ghost label ('Swap clip for #3 <-> #5') or offer a real reorder mode.

ux_friction.issue: Server preview has no scrubbable timeline tying frames back to blocks
ux_friction.evidence: variant-preview-player.tsx:380-392 renders a plain <video controls>; there is no marker overlay mapping playback position to the editable blocks, so a user who spots a problem must guess which block to fix.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Overlay block boundaries / clickable chapter markers on the server-preview scrubber.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Instant preview mounts one <video> per unique source video, all eagerly created
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: timeline-editor.tsx:1106-1125 maps over uniqueSourceVideoIds and renders a <video> for each; a bulk video drawing on 30-50 distinct source clips creates 30-50 simultaneous media elements + decoders. preload is 'auto' only for active+next (236-241) but all elements still exist and hold network/decoder handles.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: On a long timeline with many sources, browser hits the per-page media-element/decoder limit (~16-75 depending on platform), causing failed loads, audio desync, or tab crashes — exactly the 'bulk production' case the product targets.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Server preview render is a full FFmpeg job behind a 5-min timeout and a single preview slot
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: pipeline_routes.py:5729 acquire_preview_slot() serializes preview renders; each is a real assemble_and_render_preview producing a 540x960 file (5736-5766). At 10x users on one desktop sidecar, previews queue head-of-line behind each other and full renders.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: Multiple variants/users reviewing in parallel serialize into a 30-80s-each queue; the in-memory _pipelines dict + per-pipeline locks also mean a backend restart drops all in-flight preview state (consistent with the known in-memory-pipeline pain point).

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Whole-timeline fingerprint invalidates on any single-block edit
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: matches_fingerprint hashes the entire match_overrides array + all visual settings (pipeline_routes.py:5650-5654); changing one segment's duration_override or swapping one clip changes the hash, forcing a full re-render of the entire video for the server preview.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: Iterative single-block edits each trigger a fresh 30-80s render with zero reuse of unchanged segments — review cost scales linearly with edit count, punishing the careful editor.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: rAF loops + 60fps segment enforcement run continuously during preview
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: timeline-editor.tsx:406-449 runs a requestAnimationFrame enforcement loop (slowed to 100ms setTimeout only when paused) and a second rAF tracking loop (327-353) while preview is active.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: Acceptable for one preview, but on lower-end seller laptops with many pooled videos the combined rAF + decode load can drop frames and heat the machine; not a server-scaling issue but a per-client one.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: matchesRef + per-render serialization of full overrides on every preview POST
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: variant-preview-player.tsx:130-146 serializes the entire matches array (including transforms blobs) on each render request; large timelines produce large payloads.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: Minor at current sizes; grows with very long scripts but unlikely to be a primary limiter.

missing_features.feature: Transforms/PiP/logo composited live into the instant (no-render) preview
missing_features.user_value: Users see the actual final look — rotation, zoom, PiP, logo, color — without a 30-80s render, closing the fidelity gap between the two preview systems and preventing wrong exports.

missing_features.feature: Drag-to-trim in/out point within a matched segment
missing_features.user_value: Pick which part of a 5s clip plays instead of only nudging total duration; essential for getting the right beat/visual and matching CapCut-level control.
