missing_features.feature: Direct on-canvas PiP/logo placement (free X/Y/size) on the video
missing_features.user_value: Drag-and-resize overlays where you want them instead of 4-corner/3-size presets — table stakes vs CapCut/Veed/Creatify.

missing_features.feature: Local image upload / product picker for interstitial slides
missing_features.user_value: Non-technical e-commerce sellers can insert their own product photos without finding a URL.

missing_features.feature: Per-cut transition picker (crossfade/whip/slide)
missing_features.user_value: Hard cuts look amateur for social; quick transition presets between blocks raise perceived production value, matching Submagic/CapCut output.

missing_features.feature: Incremental / segment-level preview caching
missing_features.user_value: Re-rendering only the changed block instead of the whole video makes iterative editing fast and cheap, removing the linear-cost-per-edit penalty.

missing_features.feature: Lazy/windowed video-element pooling in instant preview
missing_features.user_value: Keeps long timelines with many source clips from exhausting browser decoders/crashing — directly protects the bulk-production use case.

missing_features.feature: Chapter/block markers overlaid on the server-preview scrubber
missing_features.user_value: When a problem is spotted in full preview, click the marker to jump straight to the offending block to fix it.

missing_features.feature: Undo/redo for editing actions (swap, drag, duration, slide insert)
missing_features.user_value: Editing confidence — recover instantly from a mis-swap or accidental drag; expected in any editor.

missing_features.feature: Keyboard shortcuts in the timeline editor (the source player has them; the editor doesn't)
missing_features.user_value: Power users editing dozens of videos can space-to-play, arrow-to-step, and hotkey-swap without mousing — major throughput gain for high-volume creators.

missing_features.feature: Per-segment volume / ducking and background-music track
missing_features.user_value: Add music under the voiceover with ducking — a near-universal short-form requirement absent from this slice.

missing_features.feature: Persist instant-preview playback position / paused first frame on open
missing_features.user_value: Immediate visual of the opening shot and no need to re-press play after every edit; smoother review loop.

summary: This slice covers voiceover (ElevenLabs multi-account with 402 quota failover + Edge-TTS free fallback, plus Coqui/Kokoro stubs) and burned-in captions/subtitles. The voiceover engine is genuinely strong: per-profile encrypted multi-key vault, auto-failover across up to 3 accounts on quota exhaustion, TTS caching to avoid re-billing, silence trimming, and character-level ElevenLabs timestamps that drive perfectly-synced SRT generation (no Whisper needed). Caption rendering is the weak half: it is static ASS force_style burn-in (font, color, outline, shadow, glow, adaptive sizing, position) with NO word-level karaoke highlighting, NO animation/pop, and NO emoji — the exact features Submagic/CapCut/Opus built their reputation on. Voice selection UX is partial: a real voice picker exists in pipeline Step 2 (premade vs custom grouping, default-voice star) but there is no in-app voice audition, no stability/style/speed controls in the UI, and the standalone TTS Library lets you pick a model but not a voice at all. Overall maturity: voiceover solid, captions partial and visibly behind best-in-class.

features_present.name: ElevenLabs multi-account with auto-failover
features_present.description: Up to 3 encrypted API keys per profile (Fernet, derived from SUPABASE_KEY), ordered primary-first, with automatic 402 quota failover that records the error and rotates to the next key mid-generation (elevenlabs_account_manager.py + tts/elevenlabs.py:362-395). Per-account subscription/credit display with 5-min TTL auto-refresh.

features_present.name: Edge-TTS free fallback
features_present.description: Microsoft Edge neural voices, zero cost, with retry + empty-file guard and word-boundary SRT generation (edge_tts_service.py). Genuine graceful degradation when ElevenLabs unavailable — strong for the BYOAK no-cost story.

features_present.name: Character-level timestamp -> SRT subtitle generation
features_present.description: Uses ElevenLabs alignment (character_start/end_times) to build phrase-grouped SRT with perfect audio sync, max 40 chars / 7 words per cue, sentence-aware splitting, min-duration enforcement and word-count validation (tts_subtitle_generator.py). Avoids Whisper entirely on the ElevenLabs path.

features_present.name: TTS caching
features_present.description: Fingerprint cache keyed on text+voice+model+settings avoids re-billing identical generations at both raw and silence-trimmed levels (elevenlabs_tts.py:156-162, tts/elevenlabs.py:454). Directly supports the zero-per-video-cost value prop.

features_present.name: Silence trimming with timestamp remapping
features_present.description: Removes dead air from generated audio and remaps caption timestamps into the trimmed timeline so subtitles stay synced (tts_subtitle_generator.remap_timestamps_dict + silence_remover).

features_present.name: Caption styling (ASS force_style)
features_present.description: Font family/size, text/outline color, outline width, shadow depth, glow (faked via wider semi-transparent outline), adaptive font sizing for long lines, position, opacity, per-resolution scaling via original_size, and 6+ hardcoded presets including a static 'karaoke' yellow style (subtitle_styler.py, video-processing.ts).

features_present.name: Voice picker in pipeline Step 2
features_present.description: Lists provider voices grouped into custom vs premade, shows language, lets user set a per-profile default voice (star), and persists it (step2-tts.tsx:288-339). Functional but minimal.

features_present.name: TTS Library (standalone reusable assets)
features_present.description: CRUD over persisted MP3+SRT assets, batch grouping by creation time, duplicate detection, failed-asset cleanup, waveform scrubber, SRT viewer/copy, cost estimate, polling for generating status (tts-library/page.tsx). Good asset management.

features_present.name: Voice Activity Detection (Silero VAD)
features_present.description: Detects and mutes human voice in source footage so original audio doesn't fight the voiceover (voice_detector.py). Useful, but depends on torch which may not be installed in a packaged desktop build (SILERO_AVAILABLE gate).

features_present.name: Voice cloning + extra providers
features_present.description: Coqui XTTS voice cloning endpoint (tier+ML gated, 6s min sample) and Kokoro/Coqui listed as providers. Cloning is pro-gated and the cloned voice is only logged, not persisted to profile (tts_routes.py:443-446 'will be implemented'). Effectively a stub.

user_flow: In the main pipeline a creator writes an idea, gets N script variants, then in Step 2 picks a voice from a dropdown (premade vs custom groups, can star a default) and generates voiceover per variant; ElevenLabs returns audio + character timestamps that become a synced SRT, silence is trimmed, and the result is cached. In Step 3 they review captions whose look is controlled by a styling panel (font/color/outline/shadow/glow/position/adaptive) and one of ~6 presets, then render burns the SRT into the video via FFmpeg ASS. Separately, the TTS Library page lets them mass-create standalone voiceover assets (text + model only — no voice choice), audition them via a waveform scrubber, view/copy the SRT, edit text (auto-regenerates), and bulk-clean failed/duplicate assets. Multi-account ElevenLabs keys are managed in Settings; when the primary key hits its quota mid-batch, generation silently fails over to the next key.

strengths.point: Multi-account quota failover is real and automatic
strengths.why_it_matters: High-volume faceless creators and agencies running 50-500 videos routinely blow through a single ElevenLabs character quota. Auto-rotating across 3 keys mid-batch (tts/elevenlabs.py:362-395) without user intervention is a genuine differentiator most SaaS competitors don't expose because they bill you instead.

strengths.point: Captions are generated from ElevenLabs character timestamps, not ASR
strengths.why_it_matters: Submagic/Opus rely on Whisper transcription which drifts and misspells. Driving SRT directly from the TTS engine's own alignment (tts_subtitle_generator.py) yields word-perfect text and tight sync for free — a quiet but real quality edge.

strengths.point: Aggressive caching + free Edge-TTS fallback deliver on the zero-per-video-cost promise
strengths.why_it_matters: The whole BYOAK pitch ($79 once, no per-video fee) depends on not re-billing. The fingerprint cache plus a genuinely good free fallback voice path make 'generate 100 variants for $0' believable.

strengths.point: TTS Library asset management is mature
strengths.why_it_matters: Reusable voiceover assets with batch grouping, duplicate detection, waveform scrubbing and bulk cleanup is exactly the bulk-production workflow the product targets — most consumer tools force you to regenerate every time.

ux_friction.issue: No word-level karaoke highlighting or caption animation — captions are static blocks
ux_friction.evidence: subtitle_styler.py produces a single ASS force_style with PrimaryColour/Outline/Shadow only; the 'karaoke' preset (video-processing.ts:327) is just static yellow text. No \k tags, no per-word color flip, no pop/bounce. The character timestamps that would enable true word highlighting already exist in tts_subtitle_generator but are collapsed to phrase cues.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Emit ASS with \k karaoke tags or per-word \t color transitions using the already-available character_start/end times; offer 2-3 animated presets (word-by-word highlight, pop, slide-up). This is the single biggest gap vs Submagic/CapCut.

ux_friction.issue: No in-app voice audition before committing a voice
ux_friction.evidence: step2-tts.tsx voice Select (line 288) lists names only; there is no preview/play button per voice. Users must generate a full variant to hear a voice. ElevenLabs offers preview_url per voice that is never surfaced.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add a play button next to each voice that streams the ElevenLabs preview_url (or generates a cached 1-line sample), so users pick a voice in seconds instead of by trial-and-error full renders.

ux_friction.issue: TTS Library cannot choose a voice — only a model
ux_friction.evidence: tts-library/page.tsx create dialog (lines 619-633) exposes only an ELEVENLABS_MODELS dropdown; voice is whatever the profile default is. The standalone bulk-creation surface, the one built for mass production, gives no voice control.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add a voice selector (and provider toggle for Edge-TTS) to the TTS Library create/edit dialogs, mirroring Step 2.

ux_friction.issue: Voice settings (stability/similarity/style/speed) are hardcoded, not user-editable
ux_friction.evidence: elevenlabs_tts.py:112-117 hardcodes a single 'Ana Maria' voice_settings profile; generate_audio accepts overrides but no frontend control passes them. Competitors expose at least a stability/style slider and speed.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Surface stability/style/speed sliders (with sensible presets like 'expressive' vs 'stable') in Step 2 and TTS Library; they already flow through to the API.

ux_friction.issue: No emoji, no keyword emphasis, no multi-style-per-clip captions
ux_friction.evidence: SubtitleSettings (video-processing.ts:3-27) has no emoji, bold-keyword, or active-word fields. Submagic's core hook is auto-emoji + emphasized keywords. Nothing in subtitle_styler supports it.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add an optional emoji/keyword-emphasis pass (LLM tags important words) rendered with inline ASS color/size overrides.

ux_friction.issue: Captions render to a file (30-80s) rather than previewing live in the player
ux_friction.evidence: All styling goes through build_subtitle_filter -> FFmpeg burn-in; there is no CSS/canvas live overlay of the chosen style over the player. Caption tweaks require a full re-render to see.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Render captions as a CSS/DOM overlay in the preview player using the same SubtitleSettings, so style changes are instant; only burn in at final render.

ux_friction.issue: Voice list has no search/filter by language, gender, or accent
ux_friction.evidence: step2-tts.tsx splits voices into custom/premade groups but offers no text search; ElevenLabs accounts can have dozens of voices. tts_routes /voices accepts a language filter but the UI never uses gender/category beyond grouping.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add a search box + language/gender filter chips above the voice dropdown.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: ElevenLabs concurrency is unbounded per-request with 120s client timeout; no global TTS rate/concurrency limit
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: _call_elevenlabs_api (elevenlabs_tts.py:31-41) opens a fresh AsyncClient per call with no semaphore; /tts/generate is rate-limited at 20/min per IP but bulk pipeline/library generation bypasses that. A 100-variant batch fires many concurrent ElevenLabs calls.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: At 10x volume, bursts hit ElevenLabs per-key concurrency limits (429) and exhaust quotas fast; retries (tenacity 3x exp backoff) amplify load. Failover helps for 402 but not for 429 throttling.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Account-error lookup decrypts every key linearly to find which account failed
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: record_error (elevenlabs_account_manager.py:251-264) fetches all accounts and Fernet-decrypts each to match the failed key. Max 3 accounts so fine now, but it is O(n) decrypt per error.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: Negligible at MAX_ACCOUNTS_PER_PROFILE=3, but if the cap is raised for agencies it becomes a hot path doing crypto on every quota failure.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Edge-TTS uses a fixed 4-worker module-level ThreadPoolExecutor shared process-wide
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: edge_tts_service.py:19 _executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4) is a single global; all sync Edge-TTS calls across all profiles funnel through 4 threads with 120s timeouts.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: With many users/profiles on the free path simultaneously, Edge-TTS requests queue and time out at 10x concurrency; the free fallback becomes a bottleneck exactly when the paid path is failing over.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Subtitle generation re-reads/rewrites the SRT file in place for wrap-style injection on every render
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: build_subtitle_filter calls _inject_wrap_style_override_in_srt (subtitle_styler.py:396) which read_text + write_text the SRT each render.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: Minor disk churn per render; at high render throughput adds avoidable I/O and risks races if the same SRT is rendered concurrently for multiple variants.

missing_features.feature: Word-level karaoke / animated captions (active-word highlight, pop, slide)
missing_features.user_value: This is THE feature creators judge a short-form tool by; Submagic and CapCut win on it. The timing data already exists, so it's the highest-leverage addition to make captions competitive.

missing_features.feature: In-app voice preview/audition (play sample per voice)
missing_features.user_value: Lets users choose a voice in seconds instead of generating full variants; removes the single most annoying step in voice selection.

missing_features.feature: Auto-emoji and keyword emphasis in captions
missing_features.user_value: Signature Submagic engagement booster; bold/colored keywords and contextual emojis materially lift retention on Reels/TikTok.

missing_features.feature: Live caption preview overlay in the player (CSS/DOM, no re-render)
missing_features.user_value: Style tweaks become instant instead of a 30-80s render each; turns caption design from painful to fun and removes a core friction point.

missing_features.feature: Voice + provider selection and per-voice settings in the TTS Library
missing_features.user_value: The bulk-production surface currently can't pick a voice or tune stability/style/speed — agencies need per-asset voice control for multi-brand work.

missing_features.feature: Voice search/filter by language, gender, accent + favorites
missing_features.user_value: ElevenLabs libraries have hundreds of voices; without filtering the dropdown is unusable for power users.

missing_features.feature: Persist cloned voices to profile and reuse across pipeline/library
missing_features.user_value: Voice cloning is built but the cloned voice is only logged, not saved — finishing it gives faceless creators a consistent branded voice, a real HeyGen/Creatify-class capability.

missing_features.feature: Multi-language / auto-translate voiceover + captions
missing_features.user_value: E-commerce sellers targeting multiple markets want one script rendered in several languages; ElevenLabs multilingual models make this cheap to add.

missing_features.feature: Global TTS concurrency semaphore + 429 backoff/failover
missing_features.user_value: Protects bulk batches from ElevenLabs throttling and quota burn at scale, making 100+ video runs reliable rather than flaky.

missing_features.feature: Per-caption manual timing/text editor (drag cue boundaries, fix a word)
missing_features.user_value: Even perfect TTS sync occasionally needs a tweak; an inline cue editor (like Descript/Veed) prevents a full regenerate just to fix one subtitle.

summary: There are TWO parallel, unconnected batch systems. (1) Idea-based "Batch Pipeline" (app/api/batch_routes.py mounted at /pipeline/batch + frontend/src/app/batch/page.tsx): paste up to 20 ideas, a single background worker processes them strictly sequentially (script -> TTS -> SRT -> keyword match) to "ready_for_review", then the user must click Review or "Approve & Render" per item. (2) Product-feed batch (app/api/product_generate_routes.py /products/batch-generate + frontend/src/app/batch-generate/page.tsx): select products from a feed/catalog, one sequential worker runs the full 6-stage product-video pipeline per product end-to-end (including render). Both persist to JobStorage (Supabase + in-memory fallback) so state survives restart, and both isolate per-item failures with retry/resume. Maturity is solid-but-modest: it genuinely makes N videos unattended, but throughput is single-item-at-a-time, caps are tiny (20 ideas, page-limited product selection), there is no CSV/spreadsheet import, no auto-render for the idea batch, no SSE (only polling), and no cross-batch queue dashboard. It does NOT yet deliver the "make 50 videos at once" promise at competitive throughput.
user_flow: Idea batch: user goes to /batch, pastes one idea per line (max 20), sets words-per-subtitle + optional target duration, clicks "Start Batch". A background worker (single-flight asyncio.Lock, app/api/batch_routes.py:41) processes items one at a time; the page polls /pipeline/batch/{id} every 2.5s (batch/page.tsx:192) showing per-item status badges (queued/generating_script/generating_preview/ready_for_review/failed). When an item is ready, the user clicks "Review" (jumps to /pipeline?step=3) or "Approve & Render" (batch/page.tsx:317, renders variant 0 with hardcoded TikTok preset), then polls render progress per pipeline. Failed items can be re-run via "Resume failed" (batch_routes.py:323). Product batch: user is on /products, checks products (selectAllOnPage only selects the current page, products/page.tsx:373), opens a BatchSettingsDialog, POSTs /products/batch-generate, and is redirected to /batch-generate?batch_id=... which polls /products/batch/{id}/status every 2s (use-batch-polling.ts) showing a per-product card grid with progress %, an overall progress bar, a "Stop Batch" button (cancels via /jobs/{id}/cancel), and a "Retry N Failed" button that re-submits only failed product_ids as a new batch.

features_present.name: Idea-based batch pipeline (paste N ideas)
features_present.description: app/api/batch_routes.py: queues up to 20 ideas, each becomes a persisted pipeline processed to ready_for_review by a background worker. Clean per-item status model and progress string '{done}/{total} processed'.

features_present.name: Product-feed batch generation
features_present.description: app/api/product_generate_routes.py:180 /products/batch-generate runs the full single-product 6-stage pipeline per selected product, end-to-end including render. Per-product child job records make each independently pollable.

features_present.name: Partial-failure isolation
features_present.description: Both workers wrap each item in try/except that never re-raises (batch_routes.py:232, product_generate_routes.py:332 loop), so one failure does not abort the batch. Failed items keep their error message (truncated to 500 chars).

features_present.name: Resume / retry of failed items
features_present.description: Idea batch: POST /pipeline/batch/{id}/resume re-queues queued/failed/stuck items, skips ready ones (batch_routes.py:323). Product batch: 'Retry N Failed' resubmits only failed product_ids as a fresh batch (batch-generate/page.tsx:166).

features_present.name: Restart-durable batch state
features_present.description: Batches live in JobStorage with Supabase primary + in-memory fallback and eviction (job_storage.py); a half-finished idea batch can be resumed after a backend restart. This directly addresses the in-memory pipeline-dict pain point for the batch path.

features_present.name: Progress visibility (polling)
features_present.description: Per-item status badges + overall progress bar + counts (completed/failed/total). Product batch shows per-product cards with % and live progress text.

features_present.name: Cancel / stop batch
features_present.description: Product batch supports cooperative cancellation: worker checks job_storage.is_job_cancelled() between products (product_generate_routes.py:334) and the UI has a Stop Batch button.

features_present.name: Recent batches list
features_present.description: batch/page.tsx right rail lists the 10 most recent idea batches with status/progress and lets you re-open one. Note: list_batches over-fetches 200 jobs then filters in Python (batch_routes.py:305).

features_present.name: Shared batch settings
features_present.description: BatchSettings (provider, voice_id, words_per_subtitle, target_duration, denoise/sharpen/color for products) applied uniformly to every item. Reasonable for uniform output.

features_present.name: Buffer bulk publish (downstream of batch)
features_present.description: app/api/buffer_routes.py /bulk-publish + buffer_service.py staggers multiple rendered clips to TikTok with interval scheduling and per-clip caption fallback from DB. This is the closest thing to a publishing queue.

features_present.name: batch-upload-queue component
features_present.description: frontend/src/components/batch-upload-queue.tsx is a 6-line null placeholder ('component removed, file kept to prevent Turbopack phantom dependency error'). Dead artifact; no upload-queue UI exists.

features_present.name: Auto-render in idea batch
features_present.description: Idea batch stops at ready_for_review; rendering is a manual per-item click with a hardcoded 'TikTok' preset (batch/page.tsx:325). No 'generate AND render all' option, no per-item preset choice.

strengths.point: Real partial-failure resilience + resume
strengths.why_it_matters: For mass production the #1 fear is 'item 37 of 50 crashed and killed the run'. Both workers isolate failures and offer resume/retry of only the failures, which is the correct, competitive behavior and better than naive scripts.

strengths.point: Batch state persisted in JobStorage (Supabase + memory fallback)
strengths.why_it_matters: Long batches survive a backend restart and can be resumed — a meaningful differentiator given the rest of the app keeps pipeline working state in a volatile in-memory dict.

strengths.point: Sequential-by-design throttling respects the FFmpeg semaphore
strengths.why_it_matters: Running one item at a time avoids oversubscribing CPU/GPU/TTS on a single desktop, so batches don't thrash a laptop. It's a defensible default even though it caps throughput.

strengths.point: Product-feed batch is true one-click mass production
strengths.why_it_matters: Select products from a feed -> N finished videos with no per-item interaction. This is the closest the app gets to the core 'bulk from my catalog' promise and maps directly to the e-commerce-seller audience.

strengths.point: Clean, legible status model and per-item progress UI
strengths.why_it_matters: Both pages give clear queued/processing/ready/failed states with inline errors, so a non-technical user can see exactly what happened to each video without logs.

ux_friction.issue: Two disconnected batch UIs with no shared queue / history dashboard
ux_friction.evidence: /batch (idea, batch_routes.py) and /batch-generate (product, product_generate_routes.py) are entirely separate flows; batch-generate only shows a batch if batch_id is in the URL and has no list of past product batches.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Build one unified 'Queue' page listing ALL batches (idea + product) with status, counts, throughput, and re-open; persist product batch history the way idea batches already are.

ux_friction.issue: Idea batch caps at 20 and product selection is page-limited
ux_friction.evidence: BatchCreateRequest max_length=20 (batch_routes.py:57), MAX_IDEAS=20 (batch/page.tsx:30); products selectAllOnPage only selects the current page (products/page.tsx:373).
ux_friction.fix_idea: Raise/remove the idea cap (chunk internally), add a true 'select all N matching' for products, and show 'X of Y total selected' so a 200-SKU seller can launch the whole catalog.

ux_friction.issue: No CSV / spreadsheet import for ideas or per-row settings
ux_friction.evidence: No csv/xlsx parsing anywhere in batch pages or routes (grep found none); ideas come only from a free-text textarea, products only from a connected feed.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add CSV/Sheets upload mapping columns to idea, voice, duration, CTA, caption, schedule — this is table-stakes for OpusClip/Pictory-style bulk and is the most-requested mass-production input.

ux_friction.issue: Idea batch requires manual Review/Render per item — no auto-finish
ux_friction.evidence: Items end at ready_for_review; user must click 'Approve & Render' per item with a hardcoded TikTok preset (batch/page.tsx:317-330).
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add a 'render all on completion' toggle and a default preset per batch so 50 ideas become 50 finished files unattended; keep manual review as opt-in.

ux_friction.issue: Throughput is one item at a time; long batches feel stalled
ux_friction.evidence: _batch_worker_lock single-flight (batch_routes.py:41) and product loop awaits each _generate_product_video_task sequentially (product_generate_routes.py:376); no ETA or per-item timing shown.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Allow a small configurable concurrency (e.g. 2) gated by the FFmpeg semaphore, and surface ETA / throughput (items/min) so the user trusts a 50-item run is progressing.

ux_friction.issue: Idea-batch cancellation is absent (only product batch can stop)
ux_friction.evidence: /pipeline/batch has no cancel endpoint; only product batch checks is_job_cancelled and has a Stop button (product_generate_routes.py:334, batch-generate/page.tsx:228).
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add a cancel endpoint + Stop button to the idea batch worker mirroring the product path.

ux_friction.issue: Render progress tracking is brittle and per-tab
ux_friction.evidence: approveAndRender stores renderStates in component state with setTimeout polling (batch/page.tsx:148,302); navigating away or refreshing loses render tracking even though the render continues server-side.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Persist render jobs to the batch record and rehydrate render state on load, like the item statuses already are.

ux_friction.issue: No EventSource/SSE — fixed-interval polling only
ux_friction.evidence: use-batch-polling.ts header TODO admits the SSE endpoint was never built; idea batch polls every 2.5s, product every 2s.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add /batch/{id}/stream SSE (TODO already scoped) to cut latency and request volume for large grids.

ux_friction.issue: Output of a finished batch is not surfaced as downloadable results in one place
ux_friction.evidence: Idea batch 'Done' just shows a filename string (batch/page.tsx:531); product batch links to /librarie generally, not to this batch's outputs.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add a per-batch results view with thumbnails, bulk download/zip, and a 'send all to publish queue' action.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Strictly sequential processing across the whole backend process
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: _batch_worker_lock asyncio.Lock allows one idea-batch worker process-wide (batch_routes.py:41); product loop awaits each item (product_generate_routes.py:376).
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: At 10x (50 videos), wall-clock = sum of all items; a 50-item idea batch at ~60-80s preview each is 50-65 minutes before anything is render-ready, and only ONE batch can run at a time per backend — a second user/profile's batch is fully serialized behind the first.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Batches run in FastAPI BackgroundTasks, not a durable worker/queue
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: background_tasks.add_task(_run_batch,...) (batch_routes.py:272) and same for product batch:245; no Celery/RQ. CLAUDE.md confirms BackgroundTasks-based job system.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: A crash/restart mid-batch leaves the worker dead; idea batch needs a MANUAL /resume click and product batch has no resume at all — at higher volume this means frequent half-finished runs needing human intervention, undermining 'walk away' bulk.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: list_batches over-fetches and filters in Python
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: storage.list_jobs(limit=200) then Python filter by job_type=='pipeline_batch' then slice (batch_routes.py:305-308).
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: As total jobs grow, recent-batches list pulls 200 rows per refresh per profile and may miss older batches; no job_type filter at the DB layer means O(all-jobs) scans.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Batch status recomputed by N+1 child-job reads on every poll
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: get_batch_status loops products and calls job_storage.get_job(child_id) per product each poll (product_generate_routes.py:286-296), polled every 2s.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: A 50-product batch = 50 child-job lookups every 2 seconds per open tab; with several concurrent batches this multiplies DB/round-trip load and can throttle Supabase.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: In-memory progress + render tracking is not multi-instance safe
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: buffer_routes _publish_progress dict (buffer_routes.py:103) and batch render tracking live in process memory; CLAUDE.md notes _generation_progress dicts are in-memory only.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: Fine for single desktop, but any move to a shared/multi-worker backend breaks progress visibility; also memory grows with batch volume until eviction caps kick in (_MAX_PROGRESS_ENTRIES=1000).

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: No backpressure / disk-space guard surfaced at batch level
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: check_disk_space exists in single-product path (product_generate_routes.py:502) but the batch layer doesn't pre-check capacity for N outputs.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: A 50-video catalog run can fill the disk mid-batch on a creator's laptop, failing later items with cryptic errors rather than warning up front.

missing_features.feature: CSV / Google Sheets bulk import (idea + per-row overrides: voice, duration, CTA, caption, schedule)
missing_features.user_value: A 200-SKU seller or agency can launch an entire content calendar from a spreadsheet in one paste/upload — the canonical mass-production input every competitor offers.

missing_features.feature: Unified Batch/Queue dashboard across idea + product batches with history, ETA, throughput, and re-open
missing_features.user_value: One place to see everything in flight and past runs; trust that a long run is progressing and resume/inspect any batch.

missing_features.feature: 'Generate AND auto-render all' for the idea batch with default preset/aspect per batch
missing_features.user_value: Turns 50 ideas into 50 finished files with zero per-item clicks — the actual 'walk away, come back to finished videos' promise.

missing_features.feature: Bounded concurrency (e.g. 2-4 parallel items) gated by the FFmpeg semaphore, with throughput/ETA display
missing_features.user_value: Cuts a 50-video run from ~1 hour to a fraction while still protecting the machine; makes bulk feel fast vs OpusClip-style cloud throughput.

missing_features.feature: Durable queue with auto-resume on restart (no manual resume click), for BOTH batch types
missing_features.user_value: Crashes/restarts self-heal; product batches gain the resume the idea batch already has — essential for unattended large runs.

missing_features.feature: Per-batch results view: thumbnail grid, bulk download/ZIP, and 'send all to publish queue'
missing_features.user_value: Closes the loop from bulk generation to bulk export/publishing without hunting in the Library one file at a time.

missing_features.feature: Batch cancel + pause/resume for the idea batch
missing_features.user_value: Stop a misconfigured 20-idea run immediately instead of waiting it out; parity with the product batch's Stop button.

missing_features.feature: Per-row / per-item setting overrides (different voice, duration, hook, aspect ratio per item)
missing_features.user_value: Real campaigns aren't uniform; letting each row differ unlocks A/B hooks and multi-platform aspect ratios in one batch.

missing_features.feature: Scheduled / recurring batch runs (cron) tied directly to feed updates
missing_features.user_value: 'Every morning, make a video for each new product in my feed' — true set-and-forget automation for sellers.

missing_features.feature: SSE streaming for batch progress (TODO already scoped in use-batch-polling.ts)
missing_features.user_value: Instant progress updates and far less request load for large grids; smoother feel.

missing_features.feature: Pre-flight estimate + disk/quota guard before starting a large batch
missing_features.user_value: Warns 'this will make 50 videos, ~3.2GB, est. 40 min' up front so the user isn't surprised by a half-failed run.

summary: The render engine is a multi-stage FFmpeg pipeline: per-segment extraction/normalization (cached, GPU-decode aware) → concat (stream copy) → final encode with scale/crop, optional denoise/sharpen/color, burned subtitles, and two-pass loudnorm audio. It is mature and reliable on correctness (zombie-safe subprocess runner, disk pre-check, cancellation registry, per-segment cache, graceful CPU fallback), but its throughput ceiling is self-inflicted: every production render defaults to CPU-only VBR 2-pass (encoding_presets.py:45,69-70) and the global render semaphore is hard-pinned to 1 (ffmpeg_semaphore.py:28), so N variants encode strictly serially at ~2x single-pass cost on CPU even on machines with NVENC. NVENC is detected and used for segment extraction and previews but deliberately bypassed for the final encode. Progress is fake (step-based percentages, no ffmpeg -progress parsing), there is no HEVC/AV1 path, and no advanced NVENC rate-control tuning. For a product whose entire differentiator is "bulk local production at zero per-video cost," the encode tier is the throughput bottleneck that most limits videos/hour.

features_present.name: Multi-stage assemble pipeline (extract → concat → encode)
features_present.description: assemble_video extracts each timeline segment in parallel (throttled), normalizes to 1080x1920 (preview 540x960) CFR 30fps with consistent timescale, concats via demuxer stream-copy, then _render_with_preset does the final encode with filters+subtitles+audio (assembly_service.py:1448-1772, library_routes.py:4387-4771).

features_present.name: Per-segment extraction cache (F2)
features_present.description: Content-addressed cache keyed on source path+in/out+duration+loop+transforms+codec+fps means an iterative single-segment edit only re-extracts the changed segment; logs hit/miss counts (assembly_service.py:1545-1644). Real throughput win for edit-render loops.

features_present.name: GPU-accelerated decode + NVENC for prep/preview
features_present.description: is_nvenc_available() cached; -hwaccel cuda applied to extraction when NVENC present (assembly_service.py:1492); prep and preview codec params use h264_nvenc p1/p4 when available (ffmpeg_semaphore.py:285-314).

features_present.name: FFmpeg concurrency control (4 semaphores)
features_present.description: Separate global gates for final render (1), prep (2), preview (2), preview-prep (3), all lazily bound to the event loop with a race-safe init lock and acquire timeout (ffmpeg_semaphore.py:28-130). Prevents OOM, isolates previews from production queues.

features_present.name: Zombie-safe subprocess runner + live cancellation registry
features_present.description: safe_ffmpeg_run uses Popen+communicate with kill-and-drain on timeout (ffmpeg_semaphore.py:137-196); ffmpeg_registry tracks live processes per job_key via ContextVar so kill_job can terminate in-flight encodes cross-platform (ffmpeg_registry.py).

features_present.name: Platform encoding presets with VBR/CRF/2-pass + loudnorm
features_present.description: Pydantic-validated presets for TikTok/Reels/Shorts/Generic with -14 LUFS two-pass loudnorm, profile/level, GOP, bitrate; to_ffmpeg_params builds CPU/GPU commands; DB preset overrides merged at render time (encoding_presets.py, library_routes.py:4570-4604).

features_present.name: Two-pass true-peak audio normalization
features_present.description: measure_loudness then build_loudnorm_filter applied to real audio in final encode (skipped in preview) targeting -14 LUFS / -1.5 dBTP — broadcast-correct loudness, a genuine quality edge over naive concat tools (library_routes.py:4487-4538).

features_present.name: Video enhancement + subtitle burn-in
features_present.description: Ordered denoise(hqdn3d)→sharpen(unsharp, chroma-safe)→color(eq) filter chain then burned subtitles with shadow/glow/adaptive styling via build_subtitle_filter (library_routes.py:4437-4478).

features_present.name: Disk pre-check + output validation
features_present.description: check_disk_space (2GB default) before each render; post-encode assertion that output exists and is non-empty (ffmpeg_semaphore.py:321, library_routes.py:4768).

features_present.name: Multi-variant render orchestration with cancellation
features_present.description: Pipeline renders all variants via asyncio.gather, each wrapped in acquire_render_slot, with per-variant queued-cancel short-circuit before slot acquisition (pipeline_routes.py:4236-4286).

features_present.name: Preview render (real MP4, half-res ultrafast)
features_present.description: assemble_and_render_preview reuses the full pipeline at 540x960 CRF32 ultrafast, no loudnorm, dedicated preview semaphores — but still produces a server-side file rather than compositing in the player (assembly_service.py:2325-2432).

features_present.name: Render progress reporting
features_present.description: on_progress callback fires hardcoded step percentages (10/20/.../85) (assembly_service.py:1843-1849) — there is no ffmpeg -progress parsing, so the bar does not reflect real encode position and stalls on the long 2-pass step.

user_flow: A creator finishes Step 3 (timeline matched) and clicks Render. The pipeline route builds one render task per selected variant, then enters _render_all_variants: each task waits for the single global render slot (MAX_CONCURRENT_RENDERS=1), so variants encode one at a time. Inside each render, assemble_and_render runs 7 steps — TTS/SRT (often reused), fetch segments, match/timeline, then assemble_video extracts every segment (cache hits skip work), concats, and finally _render_with_preset performs a CPU two-pass encode: pass 1 analyzes video to a null sink, pass 2 re-encodes with filters, burned subtitles and loudnorm audio. The UI polls a job and shows step-based progress that jumps to ~85% then sits silent through the multi-minute 2-pass encode. When all variants finish (serially), outputs land in output/<profile>/ and clips are marked completed. A user editing one segment and re-rendering benefits from the segment cache on extraction but still pays the full 2-pass encode again.

strengths.point: Per-segment extraction cache makes the edit→render loop genuinely incremental
strengths.why_it_matters: The core differentiator is bulk/iterative production from a fixed footage library; reusing identical extractions across renders (assembly_service.py:1545-1644) is exactly the right optimization and most concat tools lack it.

strengths.point: Operationally hardened FFmpeg layer (zombie-safe runner, cancellation registry, disk pre-check, output validation)
strengths.why_it_matters: Non-technical sellers running unattended batch jobs need renders that fail loudly and never leak processes/zombies or fill the disk; this is production-grade plumbing competitors hide behind a SaaS backend.

strengths.point: Broadcast-correct two-pass loudness + chroma-safe enhancement chain
strengths.why_it_matters: -14 LUFS normalization and a locked denoise→sharpen→color order produce platform-consistent audio/video quality that rivals Premiere export, a real quality story for the $79/$149 positioning.

strengths.point: Clean separation of preview vs production semaphores and presets
strengths.why_it_matters: Previews never starve behind heavy renders, so the interactive Step-3 experience stays responsive even while a batch is encoding.

strengths.point: Graceful CPU fallback everywhere NVENC is absent
strengths.why_it_matters: BYOAK desktop users have wildly varying hardware; the engine still produces correct output on a GPU-less laptop, just slower.

ux_friction.issue: Render progress bar is fake — fixed step percentages, no real encode position
ux_friction.evidence: assembly_service.py:1843-1849 fires hardcoded pcts (70/85); no '-progress' anywhere in the codebase. Bar jumps to ~85% then freezes through the entire 2-pass encode.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Pipe ffmpeg '-progress pipe:1' and parse out_time/total_frames to drive a real percentage and live ETA; show pass 1 of 2 / pass 2 of 2 labels.

ux_friction.issue: Default 2-pass CPU encode makes every render feel slow with no user signal why
ux_friction.evidence: All presets default encoding_mode='vbr_2pass' (encoding_presets.py:45,160-242) and 2-pass forces CPU (encoding_presets.py:69-70); UI only warns '~2x longer' (render-settings-panel.tsx:88-92).
ux_friction.fix_idea: Default to NVENC single-pass VBR (or NVENC multipass) when a GPU is detected; reserve CPU 2-pass for an opt-in 'Maximum quality' toggle with an explicit time estimate.

ux_friction.issue: GPU Encoding toggle is silently disabled and confusing under the default preset
ux_friction.evidence: render-settings-panel.tsx:138-150 disables the GPU switch whenever 2-pass is selected (the default), so most users see GPU off with no actionable explanation beyond 'Disabled for VBR 2-pass'.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Surface a single 'Speed vs Quality' choice (Fast=NVENC 1-pass, Balanced, Max=CPU 2-pass) instead of exposing the 2-pass↔GPU mutual exclusion as a disabled switch.

ux_friction.issue: No per-render time/ETA estimate before committing a batch
ux_friction.evidence: Render endpoints start immediately; no duration estimation from segment count × resolution × preset. Batch of variants is fully serial (pipeline_routes.py:4280-4283).
ux_friction.fix_idea: Estimate seconds/variant from a quick benchmark of the host's encode speed and show 'N variants ≈ M min' before render, plus a running queue position.

ux_friction.issue: Whole-timeline re-encode on any single-segment change despite extraction cache
ux_friction.evidence: assemble_video re-concats and _render_with_preset re-encodes the full output every time; only extraction is cached (assembly_service.py:1545-1644, 1751-1768).
ux_friction.fix_idea: Cache the concatenated+encoded body per merge-group and only re-encode changed spans, or cache final encode keyed on the same fingerprint when only subtitles/audio changed.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Global render concurrency hard-pinned to 1
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: MAX_CONCURRENT_RENDERS default '1' (ffmpeg_semaphore.py:28); all library/pipeline/product renders share this single gate and asyncio.gather just queues on it (pipeline_routes.py:4280-4283).
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: At 10x volume (or 10 variants), renders are strictly serial — total wall time scales linearly with video count regardless of CPU cores or GPU headroom; a 20-video batch is a single-file queue.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Production encode is CPU two-pass by default, ignoring available NVENC
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: encoding presets default vbr_2pass and 2-pass forces use_gpu=False (encoding_presets.py:45,69-70); NVENC is only used for extraction/preview, never the final encode under the default.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: Throughput at 10x is gated by libx264 medium 2-pass on -threads 4 (encoding_presets.py:143); a GPU that could do 3-5x realtime sits idle for the heaviest step, capping videos/hour far below the 'zero-cost local' promise.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: libx264 thread cap of 4 leaves multi-core hosts underused
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: to_ffmpeg_params hardcodes -threads 4 (encoding_presets.py:143) and segment extraction also uses -threads 4; combined with render concurrency 1, a 16-core machine runs one 4-thread encode.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: On capable hardware throughput plateaus early; raising render concurrency or thread count would need OOM-aware autotuning that doesn't exist.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Pipeline/assembly working state and progress live in in-memory dicts
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: _assembly_jobs in-memory with eviction (assembly_routes.py:30-44); render_jobs/progress dicts in pipeline_routes; backend restart loses queued/in-flight render state.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: At scale a single restart or crash mid-batch loses queue position and progress for all in-flight variants; no durable render queue means batches cannot be safely resumed.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: No durable render queue / worker model — renders run inside the request's asyncio loop
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: Renders are awaited inside route handlers behind semaphores (library_routes.py:3683, pipeline_routes.py:4260-4281); no Celery/RQ-style queue (CLAUDE.md confirms BackgroundTasks-only).
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: Cannot scale out to multiple worker processes or a GPU pool; throughput is bounded by one process's single render slot, and long batches block within the web app lifecycle.

missing_features.feature: GPU-first encode path with NVENC single-pass/multipass as the default when a GPU is present
missing_features.user_value: 3-5x faster renders on the typical creator GPU, directly raising videos/hour — the core differentiator

missing_features.feature: Real ffmpeg -progress parsing with live percentage + ETA
missing_features.user_value: Users trust the render isn't frozen and can plan batches; eliminates the #1 perceived-slowness complaint

missing_features.feature: Adaptive render concurrency (raise MAX_CONCURRENT_RENDERS based on cores/VRAM, OOM-aware)
missing_features.user_value: Batch of N variants finishes in parallel instead of single-file, cutting total batch time several-fold on capable hardware

missing_features.feature: Durable render queue with resumable jobs (persisted to Supabase/SQLite, survives restart)
missing_features.user_value: Unattended overnight batches survive crashes/restarts and resume where they left off — essential for 50-500 SKU sellers

missing_features.feature: Final-encode result cache keyed on the existing fingerprint
missing_features.user_value: Re-rendering after a no-op or subtitle-only change is instant instead of a full re-encode

missing_features.feature: One-click 'Speed / Balanced / Max Quality' encode profile replacing the 2-pass↔GPU toggle puzzle
missing_features.user_value: Non-technical sellers pick an outcome, not codec internals; removes the confusing disabled GPU switch

missing_features.feature: HEVC/AV1 output option for smaller files at equal quality
missing_features.user_value: Faster uploads and smaller storage for high-volume libraries; future-proofs Reels/Shorts quality

missing_features.feature: Host encode benchmark + per-batch time estimate shown before render
missing_features.user_value: Users know 'this batch ≈ 12 min' before committing, enabling planning and reducing abandons

missing_features.feature: In-player/preview compositing (overlay subtitles+audio on cached segment clips client-side) instead of server re-render
missing_features.user_value: Sub-second preview after edits vs the current 30-80s server render, transforming the Step-3 edit loop

missing_features.feature: Per-stage timing + render telemetry (extract vs encode vs loudnorm) surfaced to logs/UI
missing_features.user_value: Lets power users and support diagnose slow renders and prove the local-speed advantage

summary: The library slice is the most mature, production-feeling part of Edit Factory. /librarie/page.tsx (2817 lines) is a clip-centric grid with hover-to-play video previews, multi-select bulk operations (delete, Postiz upload, schedule), a 30-day trash with restore, infinite-scroll cursor pagination, tag editing/filtering, six status filters, inline rename, per-clip download of video/SRT/audio, and a parallel Images tab. The /usage page gives reasonable BYOAK cost transparency: budget tracking, today/total spend, per-ElevenLabs-account character quotas, live Gemini connection test, and a cost history table. The cost_tracker.py service dual-logs to Supabase + local JSON with profile scoping and monthly-quota checks. Overall maturity is solid for single-user volumes, but it is organized around individual CLIPS rather than PROJECTS/campaigns, has no bulk export/zip, no analytics or sorting, and several scaling cliffs (N+1 content fetches, fully client-side filtering, total recount per page) that bite at the dozens-to-hundreds-of-projects scale this audience requires.
user_flow: A non-technical seller opens /librarie and lands on a clip grid scoped to the active profile. Clips load 50 at a time via infinite scroll. They can search by project/variant name, filter by subtitles/voiceover/Postiz status/TikTok/social/tag, hover a card to autoplay a muted preview after 500ms, click to multi-select, and use the bottom toolbar to bulk-delete, bulk-send to Postiz, or bulk-schedule (1 post/day cascade). Per clip they can play inline, download the MP4/SRT/MP3 individually, regenerate voiceover, remove audio, rename, tag, view script/caption, and tick manual QC/TikTok/downloaded-posted flags. Deleted clips go to a Trash tab with day-counters and restore. A separate Images tab manages AI-generated images with approve/publish/delete. For cost, the user navigates to /usage to set a budget, see today/total spend split by ElevenLabs vs Gemini, per-account character usage, and a recent cost-history table.

features_present.name: Clip grid with hover video preview
features_present.description: 9:16 cards (clip-hover-preview.tsx) that swap thumbnail for a muted/looping autoplay video after a 500ms hover delay, preload=none, graceful fallback on autoplay block.

features_present.name: Multi-select bulk operations
features_present.description: Set-based selection with select-all-visible, keyboard Delete/Escape, bottom toolbar for bulk delete, bulk Postiz upload, and bulk schedule. Optimistic updates with rollback.

features_present.name: 30-day soft-delete trash
features_present.description: Trash view with days-remaining badges (color-coded), restore, permanent delete, and empty-trash. Backed by /library/trash endpoints.

features_present.name: Cursor-based infinite scroll
features_present.description: IntersectionObserver sentinel + created_at cursor pagination with dedup, synchronous loading guard, abort-on-profile-switch. (page.tsx:320-350, library_routes.py:2001)

features_present.name: Tagging + tag filter
features_present.description: Per-clip ClipTagEditor with optimistic save/rollback, /library/tags dropdown, server+client tag filtering.

features_present.name: Per-clip asset download
features_present.description: Individual download of final MP4 (/library/files), SRT (/clips/{id}/srt), and TTS MP3 (/clips/{id}/audio) via blob or download query param.

features_present.name: BYOAK cost dashboard (/usage)
features_present.description: Budget input (localStorage), today/total spend split ElevenLabs/Gemini, per-account character quotas with progress bars, live Gemini connection test, balance links, cost-history table with show-all.

features_present.name: Cost tracker service
features_present.description: cost_tracker.py dual-logs to Supabase api_costs + atomic local JSON (10k rotation), profile-scoped summaries, monthly-cost and quota-check helpers, FAL/Gemini/ElevenLabs operations.

features_present.name: Thumbnail picker
features_present.description: thumbnail-picker.tsx lets user choose a clip thumbnail from matched segment thumbnails or load 6 extra candidate frames per segment, with reset-to-auto.

features_present.name: Status/QC workflow flags
features_present.description: Manual checkboxes for QC-verified, downloaded-and-posted, TikTok/Instagram/YouTube/Facebook posted, plus auto Postiz status; cards color-code by completion state.

features_present.name: Images library tab
features_present.description: Parallel grid for AI-generated images with approve toggle, bulk delete, inline/bulk publish to Postiz, schedule. (page.tsx:1320-1681)

features_present.name: Export presets
features_present.description: /library/export-presets returns profile-scoped + global presets, but the library UI never surfaces preset selection at export/download time.

features_present.name: Orphan clip self-healing
features_present.description: list_all_clips syncs orphaned rendered videos from disk (throttled 5min/profile) so manually-rendered files appear. (library_routes.py:2013-2024)

features_present.name: Cost-to-clip attribution
features_present.description: Costs are logged with job_id but the library never shows per-clip or per-project spend; usage page only aggregates by service. No way to see what a given video cost.

strengths.point: Genuinely fast asset triage UX
strengths.why_it_matters: Hover-to-play previews + multi-select + keyboard shortcuts + optimistic updates make scanning and acting on many clips feel snappy, which is exactly the loop a high-volume faceless creator lives in.

strengths.point: Robust async hygiene
strengths.why_it_matters: AbortController on profile switch, isMountedRef guards, synchronous pagination guard, optimistic-with-rollback mutations — this is rare maturity that prevents stale-data and race bugs at scale (page.tsx:170,497,912).

strengths.point: Real soft-delete safety net
strengths.why_it_matters: 30-day trash with restore and color-coded countdowns protects non-technical users from catastrophic bulk-delete mistakes, a clear differentiator vs hard-delete tools.

strengths.point: BYOAK cost transparency is above baseline
strengths.why_it_matters: Per-account ElevenLabs character quotas, live Gemini test, and balance links directly address the BYOAK fear of 'how much am I burning?' that the business model depends on.

strengths.point: Pragmatic graceful degradation in cost tracking
strengths.why_it_matters: cost_tracker falls back to atomic local JSON with corruption recovery and log rotation, so cost visibility survives Supabase outages — fits the offline-after-setup desktop promise.

ux_friction.issue: No project/campaign grouping — library is a flat clip firehose
ux_friction.evidence: page.tsx:2075 renders one flat grid of every clip; project_name is only a truncated subtitle line (2357). No way to collapse by project, see a project as a unit, or view 'all 12 variants of campaign X'.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add a Projects view mode that groups clips by project_id with collapsible sections, project-level thumbnail/count/spend, and a project filter. Critical for 50-500 SKU sellers.

ux_friction.issue: No bulk download / zip export
ux_friction.evidence: No zip/archive endpoint exists (grep for zip/ZipFile/archive in library_routes.py = none); bulk toolbar only offers delete/Postiz/schedule (page.tsx:2013-2050). Downloads are one-file-at-a-time via window.open.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add 'Download selected as ZIP' to the bulk toolbar (server streams a zip of MP4s, optionally +SRT). This is table-stakes for agencies handing off deliverables.

ux_friction.issue: No sorting or date filtering
ux_friction.evidence: Server hard-codes order_by created_at desc (library_routes.py:2038); UI exposes no sort control and no date-range filter — only the 7 boolean/enum filters.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add sort dropdown (newest/oldest/duration/name/spend) and a date-range filter; pass through to the cursor query.

ux_friction.issue: Search/filters only see already-loaded clips
ux_friction.evidence: filteredClips useMemo filters the in-memory clips array (page.tsx:400-452); search by project/variant name only matches the pages fetched so far, so a user searching for an old project gets nothing until they scroll-load it.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Push search + boolean filters to the /all-clips query (server-side) like tag already is, or load a lightweight index for client search.

ux_friction.issue: Cost dashboard shows no spend over time or per-video
ux_friction.evidence: usage/page.tsx renders only totals, today, and a flat recent-entries table; no daily/weekly chart despite BarChart3 icon import, and no per-clip cost despite job_id being logged.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Add a simple spend-over-time sparkline/bar chart and surface per-clip estimated cost on the clip card (chars*rate + frames*rate).

ux_friction.issue: Mixed Romanian/English microcopy
ux_friction.evidence: Toasts and labels mix languages: 'Regenerare voice-over în curs' (page.tsx:651), 'Verificat QC ✓' (2431), 'Imagini' tab (1316) next to English 'Library'/'Delete selected'.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Pick one locale (or add i18n); inconsistent language reads unfinished to non-technical buyers.

ux_friction.issue: Export presets exist but are invisible at export
ux_friction.evidence: /library/export-presets endpoint returns presets (library_routes.py:2720) but the download buttons (page.tsx:2188) just fetch the rendered MP4 with no preset/aspect/format choice.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Offer format/aspect options (or chosen preset) on the download action, or remove the dead endpoint to reduce confusion.

ux_friction.issue: Hardcoded Gemini cost estimate misleads budget
ux_friction.evidence: usage/page.tsx:472 shows '~$1.20 (60 frames)' as a fixed string while cost_tracker uses GEMINI_COST_PER_IMAGE=0.000007 (per-frame cost is fractions of a cent). The displayed estimate is ~100x off and will alarm BYOAK users.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Compute the per-video estimate from the actual constants, or remove the static figure.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: N+1 content + project fetch per clip page
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: list_all_clips loops repo.get_clip_content(clip['id']) for every clip and get_project per project_id (library_routes.py:2057-2068), explicitly annotated 'N+1 accepted for v13 desktop scale'.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: A 50-clip page issues 50+ extra DB round-trips; at hundreds of clips and Supabase-cloud latency, page loads and infinite-scroll appends become multi-second, defeating the snappy UX.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Full-table count on every first page + orphan sync
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: repo.count_clips runs each non-cursor load (2031) and _sync_orphan_clips scans disk (throttled 5min) before listing (2013-2024).
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: With thousands of clips the count and disk-walk add latency to the most common action (opening the library); throttle is per-process and lost on restart.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Entire clip list held in browser memory; filtering is client-side
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: clips state accumulates every fetched page (page.tsx:293) and filteredClips re-derives over the whole array on every keystroke/filter (400-452).
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: Users with hundreds of clips and many tags/subtitles accumulate large arrays + repeated re-renders; search feels laggy and memory grows unbounded across a long session.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Cost summary aggregates client-side over all rows
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: cost_tracker._get_summary_from_supabase selects every api_costs row (service,cost) and sums in Python (cost_tracker.py:267-312); get_all_entries pulls limit=1000.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: A power user generating thousands of clips accumulates tens of thousands of cost rows; the /usage page transfers and sums them all per load. Needs a DB-side aggregate/RPC (already flagged DB-10).

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Per-clip cost log writes serialize through one file lock
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: _add_entry holds self._log_lock and rewrites the full JSON file on every cost event (cost_tracker.py:232-243).
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: Under bulk render (many TTS/Gemini calls in parallel) cost logging becomes a serialized full-file rewrite hotspot; fine at desktop scale but a ceiling for batch jobs.

missing_features.feature: Project/campaign-level library view
missing_features.user_value: Sellers with 50-500 SKUs need to see and act on a product/campaign as a unit (all its variants, its status, its spend) instead of a flat clip stream — this is the single biggest gap vs how the audience actually works.

missing_features.feature: Bulk download as ZIP (MP4 + optional SRT)
missing_features.user_value: Agencies and sellers need to grab a whole batch of finished videos to hand off or upload manually; one-at-a-time download is a dealbreaker at volume.

missing_features.feature: Server-side search + sort + date filter
missing_features.user_value: Finding a specific past project among hundreds is impossible when search only matches loaded pages; sorting by date/duration/name/spend is expected of any asset manager.

missing_features.feature: Per-clip and per-project cost attribution
missing_features.user_value: BYOAK users want to know 'this 30-video batch cost me $X' and 'this video cost $Y' — costs are already logged with job_id, so surfacing them turns raw spend into actionable cost-per-deliverable.

missing_features.feature: Spend-over-time chart + budget alerts
missing_features.user_value: A simple daily/weekly spend chart and a 'you've hit 80% of budget' toast prevents the BYOAK surprise-bill anxiety that kills trust in the one-time-purchase model.

missing_features.feature: Saved views / smart collections
missing_features.user_value: Let users save filter+tag combinations (e.g. 'Ready to post', 'Needs QC') as one-click views to manage a high-volume pipeline like CapCut/Descript libraries do.

missing_features.feature: Bulk tag / bulk rename / bulk re-render
missing_features.user_value: Selection toolbar only bulk-deletes/publishes/schedules; bulk-tagging and bulk re-render (a /clips/bulk-render endpoint already exists at line 3790 but is unused in this UI) would massively speed organizing and fixing a batch.

missing_features.feature: Duplicate/near-duplicate detection in library
missing_features.user_value: High-volume generation produces visually similar outputs; flagging dupes (the codebase already has pHash for segments) helps users avoid posting repetitive content.

missing_features.feature: Storage usage / disk footprint visibility
missing_features.user_value: On a local desktop app, hundreds of rendered MP4s fill the drive; a storage meter + 'reclaim space' (the maintenance/cleanup endpoints exist but are UI-hidden) prevents silent disk-full failures.

summary: This slice covers e-commerce product video, AI image generation, content scheduling/calendar, feeds/catalog/associations, and Postiz publishing. The product-video engine is functionally solid but architecturally narrow: it generates a single-image Ken Burns clip (1080x1920, text overlays, optional sale badge, TTS voiceover, optional ElevenLabs subtitles) per product and does NOT use the creator's own local footage — the very differentiator the product is built on. Batch generation exists and is robust (per-product error isolation, cancellable, persisted to JobStorage) but runs strictly sequentially. The scheduling subsystem is the most mature back-end (V2 per-platform variant routing, jitter, Meta A/B visual versions, calendar, plan execution). AI image generation (create-image) is the most polished end-to-end UX (multi-model nano-banana-pro, logo overlay, AI captions, publish + schedule inline). Postiz is deeply woven into nearly every engine despite being flagged out-of-MVP-scope — a real scope tension. Overall: capable plumbing, but product-video is not yet first-class enough to win e-commerce sellers versus Creatify/competitors.
user_flow: E-commerce flow: User adds a product feed (RSS/XML URL) or syncs a catalog, then browses products on /products (catalog or feed tab, with search/sale/category/brand filters and pagination). For one product they click "Generate Video" -> routed to /product-video with product passed via URL query params -> pick voiceover mode (quick template vs elaborate AI), TTS provider (Edge free / ElevenLabs premium), duration (15/30/45/60s), encoding preset (TikTok/Reels/Shorts), CTA text, optional denoise/sharpen/color filters -> Generate -> polls a job through a 6-stage pipeline (setup, TTS, SRT, compose, render, library insert) -> "View in Library". For bulk: select many products via per-card checkboxes -> BatchSettingsDialog -> /batch-generate page polls batch status per product. Image flow: /create-image 3-step wizard (template/prompt -> generate -> logo/caption/publish). Schedule flow: /schedule selects rendered projects + Postiz integrations + per-platform times + jitter -> preview -> confirm plan -> plan executes and uploads/schedules to Postiz; /calendar shows a Postiz monthly calendar.

features_present.name: Product video generator (single)
features_present.description: 6-stage pipeline (product_generate_routes.py:481) composing a portrait Ken Burns video from ONE product image with text overlays, sale badge, TTS voiceover, optional ElevenLabs subtitles, then library insert. Quick-template or elaborate-AI voiceover.

features_present.name: Batch product video generation
features_present.description: batch-generate endpoint (product_generate_routes.py:180) processes selected products sequentially with per-product error isolation (never re-raises, :391), cancellable, persisted via JobStorage; UI multi-select on /products with batch settings dialog.

features_present.name: Product video compositor
features_present.description: FFmpeg compositor (product_video_compositor.py:506) with template-driven layout, zoompan Ken Burns, hex->ffmpeg color, sale-badge overlay via filter_complex, price formatting. Image-only — no video segment input.

features_present.name: AI image generation
features_present.description: create-image (1862-line page) + image_generate_routes.py (1342 lines): multi-model (nano-banana-pro default), aspect ratio, resolution, templates with localStorage persistence, logo overlay positioning, history, product-context injection. fal_image_service.py backs it.

features_present.name: AI caption generation
features_present.description: generate-caption (postiz_routes.py:840) uses Gemini with per-platform character limits, tone, language, hashtags, CTA. Pulls product context from the clip's project.

features_present.name: Content scheduling (V2 smart routing)
features_present.description: schedule_service.py:156 build_schedule_plan with per-platform variant routing, jitter (seeded), Meta A/B visual-version validation, timezone-aware; schedule_routes.py preview/plans/progress/calendar/sync endpoints. Mature.

features_present.name: Feeds / catalog / products
features_present.description: feed_routes.py CRUD + async sync with optimistic concurrency lock (:324); catalog_routes.py read views (v_catalog_products) with filters and approved-images; products page with catalog vs feed tabs.

features_present.name: Product-segment associations + PiP
features_present.description: association_routes.py upserts catalog-product<->segment links with selected_image_urls and pip-config; batch fetch avoids N+1. Exists but is disconnected from the product-video generator.

features_present.name: Postiz publishing
features_present.description: postiz_routes.py (1217 lines): status/validate/integrations/upload/bulk-upload/publish/bulk-publish/progress/post-status/bulk-delete/generate-caption. Deeply integrated despite out-of-MVP flag.

features_present.name: Calendar
features_present.description: calendar/page.tsx is a 23-line shell that renders PostizMonthlyCalendar — entirely dependent on Postiz, no native calendar.

strengths.point: Batch generation has genuine per-product error isolation and is persisted to Supabase JobStorage
strengths.why_it_matters: product_generate_routes.py:391 guarantees one bad product never kills the batch, and batch state survives a backend restart (unlike the in-memory pipeline) — this is exactly the resilience an e-commerce seller running 200 SKUs overnight needs.

strengths.point: The scheduling engine is unusually sophisticated for an MVP
strengths.why_it_matters: schedule_service.py implements per-platform posting times, seeded jitter to avoid spam-detection, and Meta A/B visual-version enforcement (:253) — features OpusClip/Pictory charge subscription tiers for. This is a strong moat if surfaced well.

strengths.point: AI image generation is a polished, self-contained end-to-end surface
strengths.why_it_matters: create-image takes the user from template->image->logo->AI caption->publish/schedule in one wizard with localStorage-remembered preferences — it is the closest thing in this slice to a delightful, complete flow and proves the team can ship best-in-class UX.

strengths.point: Graceful TTS degradation (Edge free vs ElevenLabs premium) is wired through product video
strengths.why_it_matters: A BYOAK e-commerce seller with no ElevenLabs key can still produce voiced product videos at zero marginal cost — directly supports the zero-per-video-cost positioning.

strengths.point: Feed sync uses optimistic concurrency locking
strengths.why_it_matters: feed_routes.py:324-335 prevents double-sync races on the same feed, which matters once multiple profiles or scheduled syncs hit a 500-SKU catalog.

ux_friction.issue: Product video uses ONLY a single product image (Ken Burns) — it never touches the creator's local footage, which is the product's headline differentiator
ux_friction.evidence: product_video_compositor.py:506 compose_product_video takes image_path only; product_generate_routes.py:541-567 resolves a single image and errors if absent. No segment/footage path anywhere in product_generate_routes.py.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Offer a 'use my footage' mode that pulls keyword-matched segments (or the product-segment association + PiP that already exists) so product videos look like real UGC, not a slideshow. This is the gap between 'slideshow tool' and 'kills Creatify'.

ux_friction.issue: Product is passed to the generator entirely via URL query params (title/image/price/brand)
ux_friction.evidence: product-video/page.tsx:44-49 reads everything from searchParams; handleGenerate (:131) posts to /products/{id}/generate which re-fetches anyway.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Pass only the product id and fetch full product server-side (already done at :520). URL-param passing breaks on truncation, special chars, and deep links, and shows stale data.

ux_friction.issue: No preview before committing to a full product-video render
ux_friction.evidence: product-video/page.tsx Generate goes straight to the 6-stage render; no thumbnail/storyboard preview of layout, template, or overlay positioning.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Render a fast single-frame composite preview (the compositor already builds the overlay filter) so sellers can confirm text/badge placement before a 30-80s render — critical at batch scale.

ux_friction.issue: No template/style picker in the product-video UI despite the compositor supporting multiple templates and colors
ux_friction.evidence: product-video/page.tsx exposes only CTA + filters; template_name/primary_color/accent_color come silently from profile video_template_settings (product_generate_routes.py:737-740).
ux_friction.fix_idea: Surface template thumbnails and brand-color pickers in the generate form so non-technical sellers can self-serve branding without editing profile JSON.

ux_friction.issue: Calendar page is a thin Postiz wrapper with no native fallback
ux_friction.evidence: calendar/page.tsx is 23 lines rendering PostizMonthlyCalendar; if Postiz is not configured the page is empty.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Show locally-scheduled plans (schedule_routes /calendar already returns assignments) in the calendar even without Postiz, so the calendar has value before social setup.

ux_friction.issue: Edge TTS path silently produces NO subtitles
ux_friction.evidence: product_generate_routes.py:709 comment 'Edge TTS: no timestamps, no subtitles'; UI hint at product-video/page.tsx:321 mentions it but easy to miss.
ux_friction.fix_idea: Generate subtitles for Edge via Whisper timing (the pipeline already uses Whisper elsewhere) so the free tier isn't visibly inferior — captions drive retention on TikTok/Reels.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Batch product-video generation is strictly sequential
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: product_generate_routes.py:332 'for product_job in product_jobs' awaits _generate_product_video_task one at a time; only FFmpeg itself is semaphore-throttled.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: At 10x (e.g. 200 SKUs), a batch that is ~40s/video takes >2 hours wall-clock on a single chain even though the machine could render 2-4 concurrently. The killer e-commerce use case (bulk catalog) is the slowest path.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Batch runs inside FastAPI BackgroundTasks, not a durable queue
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: product_generate_routes.py:245 background_tasks.add_task(_batch_generate_task); same pattern as the rest of the app.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: A backend restart mid-batch loses the in-flight loop (child job records persist, but nothing resumes the loop) — a 200-product overnight batch silently stalls with no auto-recovery.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Batch status updates do read-modify-write on the whole batch record per product
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: _update_batch_product_status (product_generate_routes.py:418) fetches the full batch, mutates product_jobs array, writes it all back; called on every product start and finish.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: With large batches the JSONB product_jobs array grows and every status flip rewrites the entire array — write amplification and a last-writer-wins race window grow with batch size.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Per-product image re-download on every generation with no shared cache check across batch
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: product_generate_routes.py:550-564 downloads from image_link if local_image_path missing, into images/{feed_id}; runs per product inside the sequential task.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: A 500-SKU feed re-fetches every remote image at render time rather than during feed sync, adding latency and external-bandwidth dependency to the render critical path at scale.

scaling_bottlenecks.issue: Schedule plan execution and Postiz bulk-upload are the integration choke point
scaling_bottlenecks.evidence: schedule_service.py execute_schedule_plan + postiz_routes.py bulk-upload/bulk-publish; all routed through one Postiz instance.
scaling_bottlenecks.impact: For a high-volume creator scheduling dozens of posts/day across platforms, throughput is bounded by Postiz API rate limits and the single Coolify container noted in memory — not horizontally scalable.

missing_features.feature: Footage-based product video (use creator's own clips, not just one image)
missing_features.user_value: Turns the slideshow generator into authentic UGC-style product videos — the actual reason a seller buys this over Creatify. The association/PiP system already links products to segments; connect it to the generator.

missing_features.feature: Parallel batch rendering (N concurrent product videos)
missing_features.user_value: Cuts a 200-SKU overnight batch from hours to minutes, making bulk production — the primary audience's core need — actually viable.

missing_features.feature: Per-product caption/title overrides and bulk caption generation in batch
missing_features.user_value: Sellers want each product's post text tailored; today batch shares one CTA and captions are generated one-clip-at-a-time post-hoc. Bulk AI captions tied to product data would close the loop to publish.

missing_features.feature: Storyboard/single-frame preview before render
missing_features.user_value: Lets sellers verify branding/layout once and trust the batch, avoiding 200 wasted renders with a wrong color or misplaced badge.

missing_features.feature: In-app template & brand-kit editor (logo, fonts, colors, badge style)
missing_features.user_value: Non-technical sellers can self-brand without editing profile JSON; brand consistency across a catalog is a top e-commerce requirement.

missing_features.feature: Direct Shopify / TikTok Shop / Google Merchant feed connectors
missing_features.user_value: The target audience lives in Shopify/TikTok Shop; a one-click connector (vs generic RSS/XML URL) removes the biggest onboarding wall. A merchant skill already exists in this workspace.

missing_features.feature: Auto product-image cleanup (background removal / upscaling) before composition
missing_features.user_value: Raw feed images are inconsistent; auto-cleanup makes every product video look professional and matches what Creatify/Pebblely offer.

missing_features.feature: Scheduled / recurring feed sync + auto-generate on new SKU
missing_features.user_value: A true set-and-forget engine: new product appears in feed -> video auto-generated -> auto-scheduled. This is the standing-ovation feature for a 500-SKU seller.

missing_features.feature: Native scheduling/calendar independent of Postiz
missing_features.user_value: Postiz is flagged out-of-MVP yet the calendar and much of scheduling depend on it; a native queue/calendar lets the scheduling moat ship without the Postiz dependency.
